Pak Study MCQs · pages 601-650

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Which Pakistani won 'Most Influential Woman in Islamic Business and Finance 2023′ award?

Correct Answer: Dr Shamshad Akhtar
Show explanation
Explanation

Caretaker Minister for Finance Dr Shamshad Akhtar has been honoured with the prestigious Award of the ‘Most Influential Woman in Islamic Business and Finance 2023’

Event edition: 5th

Event venue: Dubai, UAE

****

2

Who among the following organizes International Nautical Competition 2023?

Correct Answer: Pakistan Navy
Show explanation
Explanation

About International Nautical Competition 2023

Edition: Fourth

Organizer: Pakistan Navy

Venue: Karachi

Staring date 31 October

Ending date 03 November 2023

Purpose: to promote goodwill among friendly countries

******

3

In 2023, Pakistan issued commemorative coin of Rs.75 to mark 75th anniversary of diplomatic relations which which country?

Correct Answer: USA
Show explanation
Explanation

On 24 August 2023, The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) issued a commemorative coin of 75 rupees during a ceremony held in Islamabad to mark the 75th anniversary of the establishment of diplomatic relations between Pakistan and the US.

4

In 2023, Pakistan won Asian Junior Squash Under-13 title after how many years?

Correct Answer: 6 years
Show explanation
Explanation

Asian Junior Squash Under-13 title

Final held: August 2023

Final held: Dalian, China

Winner: Pakistan

Pakistan won: after 6 years (last won in 2016)

**** 

5

Which of the following welcomed first international Flight in August 2023?

Correct Answer: Skardu
Show explanation
Explanation

An aircraft carrying 80 passengers from Dubai landed at the Skardu airport on 14 August 2023 making it the first international flight to have done so.

Flight PK234 departed from Dubai International Airport and landed at Skardu airport.

 

6

Under which article National Assembly of Pakistan was dissolved in August 2023?

Correct Answer: Article 58
Show explanation
Explanation

Know about dissolution of National Assembly (Lower house) of Pakistan

Number: 15th

Date: 9 August 2023

Summary sent by: PM Shahbaz Sharif

Approved by: President Arif Alvi

Under Article: 58(1)

 

7

The AJK Legislative Assembly approved Rs____ billion budget for the fiscal year 2022-23.

Correct Answer: Rs163.7bn
Show explanation
Explanation

The Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) Legislative Assembly on June 30, 2022, approved an Rs163.7 billion budget for the fiscal year 2022-23.

AJK budget presented by AJK Finance Minister Abdul Majid Khan.

 

8

Which country is the champion of ACC men's Emerging Asia Cup 2023?

Correct Answer: Pakistan
Show explanation
Explanation
  • About ICC men's Emerging Asia Cup 2023
  • Edition: Fifth 
  • Venue: Sri Lanka
  • Winner: Pakistan (Second time) 
  • Runner-Up: India 
  • Final held: 23 July 2023
  • Total teams: 8
  • Pakistan won by: 128 runs
  • Total matches: 15
  • Player of the series: Nishant Sindhu (India)
  • Most runs made by: Avishka Fernando (Sri Lanka)
  • Most wickets taken by:  Nishant Sindhu (India)
  • *****
9

As per 2023 census, which province has least population growth rate in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: KPK
Show explanation
Explanation

2023 national census (released on 5 August 2023)

Growth rate: 2.55 per cent

Total Pakistan population: 241.49 million

Punjab: 127.68 million

Punjab growth rate: 2.53 percent

Sindh population: 55.69 million

Sindh growth rate: 2.57 percent

Balochistan population: 14.89 million

Balochistan growth rate: 3.2 percent (highest of all)

KPK population: 40.85 million

KPK growth rate: 2.38 percent

Islamabad population: 2.36 million

Islamabad growth rate: 2.81 percent

Pakistan rural population: 61.18 percent

Pakistan Urban population: 38.82 percent

33 million population increased between 2017 and 2023 

110 million population increased between 1998 and 2023 

*****

10

As per 2023 census, which province has highest population in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Punjab
Show explanation
Explanation

2023 national census (released on 5 August 2023)

Growth rate: 2.55 per cent

Total Pakistan population: 241.49 million

Punjab: 127.68 million

Punjab growth rate: 2.53 percent

Sindh population: 55.69 million

Sindh growth rate: 2.57 percent

Balochistan population: 14.89 million

Balochistan growth rate: 3.2 percent (highest of all)

KPK population: 40.85 million

KPK growth rate: 2.38 percent

Islamabad population: 2.36 million

Islamabad growth rate: 2.81 percent

Pakistan rural population: 61.18 percent

Pakistan Urban population: 38.82 percent

33 million population increased between 2017 and 2023 

110 million population increased between 1998 and 2023 

*****

11

Name the water-related treaty between Pakistan and India?

Correct Answer: Indus Water Treaty
Show explanation
Explanation

The Indus Water Treaty allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan

while

and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India

**

Indus Waters Treaty, treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan

Indus Water Treaty between India and Pakistan was brokered by World Bank


***

The Treaty was signed at Karachi by Field Marshal Mohammad Ayub Khan,

the then President of Pakistan, Jawaharlal Nehru 

the then Indian Prime Minister, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif 

The Treaty is effective from 1st April 1960 

****

********

***


12

National Education Commission 1959 was established under the headship of?

Correct Answer: S.M Sharif
Show explanation
Explanation

The National Education Commission of 1959 was established under the headship of S.M. Sharif.

13

Who was the first Education Minister of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Fazal-ur-Rehman
Show explanation
Explanation

The first Education Minister of Pakistan was Fazlur Rahman, who served from 1947 to 1958.

14

The first Islamic state was established in India in 1206 A.D by ?

Correct Answer: Qutub-ud-din-Aibek
Show explanation
Explanation

The first Islamic state was founded in 1206 by Qutb-ud-din Aibak 

He became the first Sultan of Delhi from 1206 to 1210.

After Muhammad Ghori's assassination in 1206.

  • He established the Delhi Sultanate (1206–1526),
  • And started the Mamluk Dynasty or Salve Dynasty.


Additional information:

He was born in 1150 in Turkestan

He Died on 14 November 1210 (aged 60) in Lahore

Burial in Anarkali Bazaar, Lahore

 

  • Qutb ud-Din Aibak
Battles/wars
First Battle of Tarain
Second Battle of Terrain
Battle of Chandawar
Siege of Kalinjar
Battle of Kasahrada (1197)
Battle of Jhelum (1206)



15

In Pakistan, the maximum number of newspapers are published in which language?

Correct Answer: urdu
Show explanation
Explanation

Newspapers are published in 11 languages;

most in Urdu and Sindhi

English-language publications are numerous. 


Additional Information

Jam-i-Jahan-Numa was the first Urdu-language newspaper.

Jang newspaper published in both Pakistan and UK

After the creation of Pakistan, the first newspaper was Imroze

16

The 3rd June Plan announced partition of the subcontinent into _____?

Correct Answer: 2 states
Show explanation
Explanation

The 3rd June Plan announced the partition of British India into 2 states:

  • India
  • Pakistan


Punjab and Bengal to be divided according to 3rd June Plan

This Plan also known as the Mountbatten Plan or Partition Plan 

It was the last plan for independence.

17

Who has become Most Expensive Overseas Player in The Hundred Draft 2024?

Correct Answer: Naseem Shah
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistani fast bowler Naseem Shah is now among the most expensive players in England’s Cricket ‘The Hundred’ Players Draft.

Birmingham Phoenix acquired Naseem Shah for £125,000, making him the first Pakistani to come in the most expensive category of GBP 125,000. His remuneration in Pakistani currency will be around 4.5 crore rupees. 

18

President Pakistan Asif Ali Zardari awarded Nishan-e-Pakistan to __________ on Pakistan Day 2024?

Correct Answer: Khalid bin Salman Al Saud (Saudi Arabia)
Show explanation
Explanation

President of Pakistan conferred Pakistan's highest civilian award "Nishan-e-Pakistan" on Saudi Arabia's Defence Minister on Pakistan Day 2024

President of Pakistan: Asif Ali Zardari

Saudi Defence Minister: Prince Khalid bin Salman Al Saud

Awarded on 23 March 2024

 *****

19

War of independence was started in which year?

Correct Answer: 1857
Show explanation
Explanation

The First War of Indian Independence was fought in the year 1857.

It is also known as the Indian Sepoy Mutiny of 1857.

War was fought in 1857 by Indians against the British in order to get rid of their domination.

20

Silk road connects which two countries?

Correct Answer: Pakistan and China
Show explanation
Explanation

The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

The silk road connects Pakistan and China.

The total length is approximately 1,300 kilometers, of which 887 kilometers are in Pakistan and 413 km in China.

The silk road is considered a symbol of Pakistan-China's eternal friendship

The highway meets the Pakistani-Chinese border at Khunjerab Pass

 It was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

Karakorum highway is also called N-35

****

ND23-2-2023

21

Lahore is situated on the bank of which river?

Correct Answer: Ravi
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Lahore is located on the bank of Ravi river. 
  • Lahore is the capital of the Pakistani province of Punjab.
  • Pakistan's 2nd largest city after Karachi.
  •  26th largest city in the world.
  • ****
22

Gurdeep Singh elected the first Sikh senator of Pakistan from _______?

Correct Answer: KPK
Show explanation
Explanation

Gurdeep Singh of the ruling Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf party became the first turban-clad Sikh representative from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province in Pakistan's Senate.

Gurdeep Singh secured 103 votes in the House of 145 whereas Jamiat Ulema-e Islam (Fazlur) candidate Ranjeet Singh secured just 25 votes and Asif Bhatti of the Awami National Party received 12.

23

The length between Pakistan and Afghanistan border is:

Correct Answer: 2611 KM
Show explanation
Explanation

According to the Survey of Pakistan 2021, the new length border between Pakistan and Afghanistan is 2611 km

The old length between Pak vs Afghanistan is 2430

Pakistan and Afghanistan are called the Durand Line

The Durand line was drawn in 1893 

Durand line agreement between Mortimer Durand and Abdul Rahman.


Additional information:

Pakistan's total land border is 6219KM long

Pakistan's total land border with Land Border + Sea 7277KM

Land Border + Sea + LOC & Working Boundary 8340 KM

Pakistan borders India 2100 KM long in the east

Afghanistan border is 2,611 KM long in the west (northwest)

Iran border is 909 KM long also in the west

China's long border is situated in the northeast.

The beach of the Arabian Sea 1058 km forms its southern border.

24

OGRA Stand for _______?

Correct Answer: Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority
Show explanation
Explanation

The Oil & Gas Regulatory Authority is an agency of the Government of Pakistan,

It is responsible for regulating the oil and gas sector in Pakistan.

OGRA was established on March 28, 2002 

Its headquarters is located in Islamabad.

It was founded by the Federal Government.

25

The Supreme Court's building design was prepared by famous ____firm, M/S Kenzo Tange Association.

Correct Answer: Japanese
Show explanation
Explanation

The building's design was prepared by a famous Japanese firm, M/S Kenzo Tange Associates. 

It is located on the Constitution Avenue in Islamabad, Pakistan. 

The Construction started in the 1960s

Building Completed in 1993

26

Kulbhushan Jadhav arrested on which area of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Charman
Show explanation
Explanation

Jadhav was arrested in Pakistan's restive province of Balochistan (near the Pak-Afghan border of Chaman)

He was arrested on 3 March 2016

His death sentence was suspended by the International Court of Justice

27

Nawaz Sharif park, which was renamed as Allama Iqbal Park is located in__________?

Correct Answer: Rawalpindi
Show explanation
Explanation

The Parks and Horticultural Authority (PHA) in 2019 renamed Nawaz Sharif and Shahbaz Sharif parks as Iqbal Park and Rawal Park, respectively.

List of Caretaker Prime Ministers of Pakistan

 

Number   

Name

Duration

1

Ghulam Mustafa Jatoi

6 Aug 1990 to 6 Nov 1990

2

Balakh Sher Mazari

18 Apr 1993 to 26 May 1993

Moeenuddin Qureshi

18 July to 19 October 1993

Malik Meraj Khalid

6 Nov 1996 to 17 Feb 1997

29

Kala Deer is found in which area of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Cholistan
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Kala Deer is found in Cholistan Desert. 
  • Cholistan Desert is located 30 kilometers from Bahawalpur, Punjab.
  • Locally this desert is known as Rohi.
  • Jeep Rally is also held in Cholistan Desert. 
  • Cholistan lies on the southern part of the Punjab. 
30

Gasherbrum I is also known as ___?

Correct Answer: Hidden Peak
Show explanation
Explanation

Gasherbrum is a remote group of peaks situated at the northeastern end of the Baltoro Glacier in the Karakoram mountain range.

The massif contains three of the world's 8,000 metre peaks.
31

Khilafat movement was ended by

Correct Answer: None of these
Show explanation
Explanation

The Khilafat Movement came to an end in 1924 when Mustafa Kemal Atatürk abolished the Ottoman Caliphate.

The movement started in 1919.

It was led by Indian Muslim leaders like Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar and Maulana Shaukat Ali to protect the Caliphate.

32

What is the rank of Pakistan among countries having gas reserves?

Correct Answer: 29
Show explanation
Explanation

Sindh has 124 gas fields and accounts for 63pc. The biggest field of natural gas is in the Sui region of Balochistan accounting for 6pc.

 

33

The MoUs worth of how many billion dollars signed between China and Pakistan regarding CPEC?

Correct Answer: 46 billion dollars
Show explanation
Explanation

The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) is a series of energy and infrastructure

projects, some of which connect China’s Xinjiang to Gwadar.

Initiated in mid-2013 and formally launched in April 2015.

CPEC is generally described as a $46 billion package of Chinese aid.


34

How many times Pakistan has been elected as the president of the UN Economic and Social Council?

Correct Answer: 6 times
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan's ECOSOC Presidency:

  • With Ambassador Munir Akram serving as the 76th President
  • Total Terms as President: 6 times.
  • Most Recent Term: July 2020 to July 2021 
  • Other Tenure Years: 1952, 1957, 1975, 1995, 2005.
  • Focused on COVID-19 recovery

Additional information:

  • ECOSOC stands for the United Nations Economic and Social Council
  • Comprised of 54 members (elected for overlapping three-year terms)
  • Founded on June 26, 1945

****

Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026





35

Which of the following is the most competitive exam in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: CSS
Show explanation
Explanation

 CSS is considered to be most competitive exam in Pakistan.

CSS stands for Central Superior Services.

FPSC takes examination for CSS tests annually. 



36

Bahawalpur is mostly consists of?

Correct Answer: Deserts
Show explanation
Explanation

Bahawalpur was founded in 1748 by Nawab Bahawal Khan I, after migrating to the region around Uch from Shikarpur, Sindh.

On 5 October 1947 the Nawab signed an agreement with the government of Pakistan according to which Bahawalpur State acceded to Pakistan, and the accession was accepted on 9 October. Thus the State of Bahawalpur was the first state to accede to Pakistan.

Bahawalpur is located in Punjab.

Bahawalpur mostly consists of Deserts.

Correct Answer: Anwaarul Haq Kakar

Explanation

Anwaarul Haq Kakar became first person from Balochistan to become interim prime minister of Pakistan.

Tenure: August 2023 to March 2024

Correct Answer: India

Explanation

Prime Minister Imran Khan has expressed his desire to engage Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi in a television debate in February 2022.

39

Who established the Mughal Empire?

Correct Answer: Babur
Show explanation
Explanation

Babur was the founder and first Emperor of the great Mughal dynasty in India.

Babar invaded the kingdom of the Lodi Afghans in northern India in 1526 

Mughal Empire lasted from 1526 to 1857.

There are 17 Mughal families who rule in India

First Mughal Emperor: Babar

Last Mughal Emperor: Behadur Shah Zafar


40

Instrument of accession of Kashmir was signed by?

Correct Answer: Maharaja Hari Singh
Show explanation
Explanation

Maharaja Hari Singh and the Indian government negotiated the terms of the Instrument of Accession

Signed 26 Oct 1947

Effective on 27 Oct 1947

This document allowed Jammu and Kashmir to join India.

Explanation

برطانوی حکومت نے تین جون 1947 کو برصغیر کی تقسیم کا اعلان کیا تھا۔

42

Syed Ahmed Shaheed along with Shah Ismail were martyred in a battle with Sikhs at:

Correct Answer: Balakot
Show explanation
Explanation

Sayyid Ahmad Shaheed was an Indian Muslim revivalist from Rae Bareli, a part of the historical United Provinces of Agra and Oudh.

Sayed Ahmad Shaheed died 06 March 1831 at Balakot

He is considered as a scholarly authority by Ahl-i Hadith and Deobandi movements

مارچ 1940 میں مسلم لیگ کا سالانہ اجلاس ــــــــ جگہ منعقد ہوا تھا۔

Correct Answer: Manto Park, Lahore

44

Pakistan beat ______________ to secure bronze medal at Hockey Champtions Trophy 2012.

Correct Answer: India
Show explanation
Explanation

Pakistan defeated India to win bronze Champions Trophy 2012, held in Melbourne, Australia.

Pakistan is the most successful national field hockey team in the Hockey World Cup.

 Pakistanis the four-time champion: 

1971

1978

1982

1994.

***

Pakistan defeats Spain in the 1971 hockey world cup.

Pakistan beat India to secure the bronze medal at Hockey Champions Trophy 2012.


Pakistan played its first International Hockey matches in 1948

In 1994 year, Pakistan was the world champion of Hockey

In 1994 en Pakistan won the World Hockey Cup last time




45

The gandhara civilization followed the religion:

Correct Answer: Budhism
Show explanation
Explanation

Gandhara Civilization flourished in Pakistan during the rule of Buddhist Kings.

  • Taxila was considered as Pearl of Gandhara Civilization. 
  • language of the Gandhara civilization was:  Prakrit
  • ***
  • Gandhara's language was a Prakrit or "Middle Indo-Aryan" dialect, usually called Gāndhārī.
  • Hindko from Peshawar, which was the capital of Gandhara, came from Shuraseni Prakrit a language spoken in Gandhara.


46

Against whom the Muslim ruler and Warrior Salahuddin Ayubi fought?

Correct Answer: Crusaders
Show explanation
Explanation

Crusaders صلیبی

Salah ud Din Ayubi from Kurd generation.

Sultan Salahuddin Ayubi was the legendary warrior who fought the Crusaders for a long time and

at last, overcame them and gained victory before he pushed back the joined powers of Europe away from the Holy Land.

سلطان صلاح الدین ایوبی وہ عظیم جنگجو تھے جنہوں نے طویل عرصے تک صلیبیوں کا مقابلہ کیا اور

آخر کار، ان پر قابو پا لیا اور فتح حاصل کی اس سے پہلے کہ اس نے یورپ کی مشترکہ طاقتوں کو مقدس سرزمین سے پیچھے دھکیل دیا۔



47

Ayoub National park is situated in?

Correct Answer: Rawalpindi
Show explanation
Explanation

Ayub National Park named after the Pakistani president and general Ayub Khan.

The park was established before the creation of Pakistan and covers an area of 313 acres (127 ha).

its claimed to be the Asia's largest park

Which of the following countries are divided by the Radcliffe Line?

Correct Answer: India and Pakistan

Explanation

49

He was an expert in Islamic Laws (Quran and sunnah)?

Correct Answer: Rana Bhagwan Das
Show explanation
Explanation

Rana Bhagwandas was born on 20th December 1942 into a Hindu Sindhi Rajput family in Naseerabad, Larkana District in Sindh.

He studied Law and received a Master’s degree in Islamic Studies. 

He was a senior judge and former acting Chief Justice of the Supreme Court of Pakistan during 2007.

He became the first Hindu and the third non-Muslim (after A.R Cornelius and Dorab Patel) 

He was also a member of the selection board of the Sindh Madressatul Islam University Karachi.



50

Marala Barrage was constructed on the which river?

Correct Answer: Chenab
Show explanation
Explanation

Marala Headworks is a headworks situated on the Chenab River near the city of Sialkot in Punjab.

 A weir was first built during 1906–1912

شملہ کانفرنس منعقد ہولی

Correct Answer: 1945

Explanation

    Simla Conference Presided by Lord Wavell

    The Simla Conference of 1945 was a meeting between:

    the Viceroy of India Lord Wavell and the major political leaders of British India

    at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla.

    ****

    The Shimla agreement was signed in 1972

      The Simla Agreement was signed by Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and President Zulfikar Ali Bhutto of Pakistan.

      52

      قیام پاکستان کے بعد کی زبان کو قومی زبان قرار دیا گیا

      Correct Answer: اردو
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      پاکستان کے 1973 کے آئین میں اردو کو سرکاری طور پر پاکستان کی قومی زبان قرار دیا گیا تھا۔


      اردو پاکستان کی قومی زبان ہے۔

      یہ فارسی، عربی اور مختلف مقامی زبانوں کا مرکب ہے

      ND7-1-2023


      53

      پاکستان کا پہلا آئین کس سن میں پیش کیا گیا ؟

      Correct Answer: 23rd March 1956
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Constitution of 1956 was the fundamental law of Pakistan from March 1956 until 1958 Pakistani


      پاکستان کو 9 سال بعد پہلا آئین ملا

      پاکستان نے 14 اگست 1947 کو آزادی حاصل کی اور 23 مارچ 1956 کو پہلا آئین حاصل کیا۔

      1956 Constitution was consist of 234 articles 13 parts and 6 schedules.

      سکندر مرزا نے پاکستان کا پہلا آئین اکتوبر 1958 کو منسوخ کیا تھا۔

      ND7-1-2023


      54

      تقسیم ہند کے وقت برصغیر میں کتنی دیسی ریاستیں تھیں ؟

      Correct Answer: 635
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The largest Princely state by area at the time of partition was Kashmir.

      • Out of 565 or 635 princely states during 1947, 13 princely states decided to join Pakistan

      55

      لال پور کا اب نام کیا ہے

      Correct Answer: فیصل آباد
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      یکم ستمبر 1977 کو حکومت پاکستان نے سعودی عرب کے شاہ فیصل کے اعزاز میں شہر کا نام لائل پور سے بدل کر فیصل آباد ('فیصل کا شہر') رکھ دیا

      Faislabad is the 3rd largest city in Pakistan after Karachi and Lahore

      ND1-1-23

      56

      بنگال کی تقسیم کب ہوئی

      Correct Answer: 1905
      Show explanation
      Explanation


      تقسیم بنگال کی تنسیخ سے مایوسی سب سے زیادہ مسلمانوں کو ہوئی۔

      تقسیم بنگال 1905 میں ہوی

      تنسیخ 1911 میں ہوئی

      ***

      ND5-01-2023

      57

      جنگ پلاسی کب ہوئی

      Correct Answer: 1757
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      پلاسی کی جنگ 23 جون 1757 کو شمال مشرقی ہندوستان میں لڑی گئی۔

      پلاسی کی جنگ بنگال کے نواب اور اس کے فرانسیسی اتحادیوں پر برطانوی ایسٹ انڈیا کمپنی کی فیصلہ کن فتح تھی۔

      ND1-1-23

      58

      The Naval Headquarters is located in ________, Pakistan ?

      Correct Answer: Islamabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Pakistan Navy is the naval branch of the Pakistan Armed Forces

      the Headquarters Pakistan Navy in Islamabad

      the Founded Pakistan Navy on August 14, 1947

      the Chairman Joint Chiefs of Staff of Pakistan Navy: General Sahir Shamshad Mirza

      Gifford James was the first C-in-C of the Pakistan Navy

      Admiral Mohammad Siddique was the first Muslim C-in-C of the Pakistan Navy.

      ND2023-2-12


      **********

      VU CBT FIA PAPER 25-12-2021
      59

      Where is the Pakistan largest Mosque (Masjid) and the world 3rd largest in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Karachi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Grand Jamia Mosque, Karachi also known as Bahria Town Jamia Masjid Complex is a cultural complex under construction in Bahria Town Karachi, Pakistan.

      When completed, the complex will include Pakistan's largest and world's third largest mosque according to capacity.

      60

      Which city of Pakistan is known as Little Manchester?

      Correct Answer: Faisalabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Faisalabad is called Manchester of Pakistan

      Because it's the biggest textile city in Pakistan

      It is formerly known as Lyallpur

      It is the third-largest city in Pakistan

      It is the second largest city in the Province of Punjab.

      It is famous for its textile mills


      61

      Punjab is famous for what?

      Correct Answer: Land of fiver Rivers
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      1. Punjab is made of two words Punj (Five) + Aab (Water) i.e. land of five rivers.

        These five rivers of Punjab are:

        Sr#
        River Name
        Province
        Length Final
        1
        Sutlej River
        Punjab
        1450 km
        2
        Chenab River
        Punjab
        960 km
        3
        Jhelum River
        Punjab
        725 km
        4
        Ravi River
        Punjab
        720 km
        5
        Beas River
        Punjab
        250 km
      62

      Rakaposhi Mountain is the:

      Correct Answer: 27th highest mountain in the world
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Name: Rakaposhi Mountain

      Ranking: 27th highest mountain globally

      Location: Situated in the Karakoram range, Pakistan

      63

      Which of the following fort was built by Akbar?

      Correct Answer: A & B
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Attock Fort and Lahore Fort were built by Emperor Akbar.

      Lahore Fort (It is also known as Shahi Qila

      Initiated the construction in the 1560s)

      *

      Attok Fort (constructed in 1581

      Construction was completed in two years 1583)



      Additional information 

      Lahore fort:

      The Lahore Fort is located in the northern part of Lahore's old walled city.

      The fort's Alamgiri gate is part of an ensemble of buildings, which along with the:

      • Badshahi Mosque
      • Roshnai Gate
      • and Samadhi of Ranjit Singh


      Attok fort:

      It is sandwiched between Peshawar Road on one side and the River Indus on the other.

      The fort consists of 4 gates.

      • Delhi gate
      • Lahori Gate
      • Kabuli Gate 
      • Mori Gate


      Last updated 15-03-2026

      64

      Which region does the N-35 road connect Gilgit to?

      Correct Answer: China's Xinjiang
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Karakoram Highway is a 1,300 km national highway

      It extends from:

      Hasan Abdal in the Punjab province of Pakistan

      to the Khunjerab Pass in Gilgit-Baltistan,

      where it crosses into China

      and becomes China National Highway 314.

      65

      How many major mountain ranges are there in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 4
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan has four major mountain ranges:

      1. Himalaya,
      2. Karakoram,
      3. Hindu Kush,
      4. Sul-e-man Range
      66

      Clifton is in which city of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Karachi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Clifton is an upscale and historic seaside locality in Karachi, Pakistan.

      Clifton was initially developed in the late 19th century under British colonial rule

      There are also several international consulates and high commission offices based in Clifton such as:

      British Deputy High Commission

      The Chinese consulate

      The Italian Consulate

      The Russian Consulate

      The Iranian Consulate

      Consulate General of Switzerland

      ND-5-09--023

      67

      Famous shrine of Hazrat bari imam is located at :

      Correct Answer: Islamabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      A silver-mirrored shrine of Bari Imam is located in Noorpur Shahan in Islamabad.

      Born 1617 CE Choli Karsal, Punjab, Mughal Empire

      Died 1705 CE Noorpur, Punjab, Mughal Empire


      68

      Who is the first-ever female Colonel Commandant of the Army Medical College?

      Correct Answer: Nigar Johar
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      •  Nigar Johar is the first-ever female Colonel Commandant of the Army Medical College
      • **
      • Chief of Army Staff (COAS) General Qamar Javed Bajwa in a break with precedent has installed Lt General Nigar Johar “as first-ever female general to be appointed as Colonel Commandant of Army Medical Corps (AMC)”
      • ***

      • Major General Nigar Johar, HI (M) promoted to Lieutenant General.
      • The officer has been appointed as 1st female Surgeon General of the Pak Army.
      • ***
      • The date of Birth of Major General Nigar Johar is January 01, 1970
      • The age of Major General Nigar Johar is 52 Years
      • Birth Place of Major General Nigar Johar  is Panjpir, Pakistan
      69

      Pakistan area wise position in the world

      Correct Answer: 33
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan is the 33rd-largest country by area, spanning 881,913 square kilometers.

      The total cultivated area in Pakistan is 25%

      The total forest area of Pakistan is estimated at  6.20 million hectares

      The forest cover of Pakistan is 5.7%.

      the total area of Pakistan is 881,913 km² 


      70

      Which country assisted Pakistan in the construction of Sandak Project?

      Correct Answer: China
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Saindak was made in the 1970s in collaboration with a Chinese engineering firm.

      Large deposits of copper and gold have been discovered in Saindak

      The project was started in 1995 by the Government of Pakistan.

      This Mine is located near Saindak town in the Chagai Balochistan



      71

      National Code of Pakistan is:

      Correct Answer: PK
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      County codes were introduced in 1960 by the International Telecon Union.

      PK is the national code of Pakistan.

      +92 is the dialling code of Pakistan. 

       

       
      72

      Pakistan is the winner of Hockey World Cup in ________ ?

      Correct Answer: All of these
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      First Hockey World Cup was played in 1971 at:

      Barcelona

      Azlan Shah Cup is related to which among the following Sports

      Hockey

      Current (2018) Hockey World Cup was be held in?

      India

      Current (2018) Hockey World Cup winner

      Belgium

       

       

      73

      Maulvi Tameez-ud-Din was -------?

      Correct Answer: Speaker and president of Pakistan's Constituent Assembly
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Tameez Ud Deen was the Speaker and president of Pakistan's Constituent Assembly

      ***


      Maulvi Tamizuddin Khan was the Speaker of Pakistan's Constituent Assembly from 1948 to 1954

      and the National Assembly of Pakistan between 1962 and 1963.

      1st Deputy Speaker of the National Assembly of Pakistan from 23 February 1948 – 13 December 1948

      ****
      Born March 1889 Faridpur, Bengal Presidency, British India

      Died           19 August 1963 (aged 74) Dacca, East Pakistan, Pakistan

      Khan joined the non-cooperation movement led by Gandhi when he was a student.

      Later he joined the Indian National Congress and subsequently joined the khilafat movement in 1921

      ***

      ND2-12-2022

      74

      What are the pointed shoes usually worn on Pakistan weddings called ?

      Correct Answer: Khussa
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Khussa is a collage of all the syncretic influences of Pakistani culture, made from leather, it is embroidered with traditional motifs and mirror work.

      75

      Hayat e javeed is written by

      Correct Answer: Altaf Hussain Hali
      Show explanation
      Explanation


      Hayate-e-Javed is written by Altaf Hussain Hali about Sir Sayyid Ahmad Khan.

       He got it published twice- first in 1901 and then in 1903.

      76

      Who were defeated in the third battle of Panipat in 1761?

      Correct Answer: Marhathas
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Afghans defeated Marhathas in the 3rd battle of Panipat. 

      1st battle of Panipat: 1526

      2nd battle of Panipat: 1556

      3rd battle of Panipat: 1761

      77

      Raja Dahar was the ruler of?

      Correct Answer: Sindh
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Raja Dahir was the last Hindu ruler of Sindh, in present-day Pakistan, in the western region of the Indian subcontinent.

      78

      Allama Iqbal died at the age of ______.

      Correct Answer: 60 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Know about Allama Iqbal

      Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)

      Father: Sheikh Noor Muhammad

      Mother: Imam Bibi

      Received PhD: Germany

      Title: Poet of the East

      Famous books: Bang-e-Dara, Zarb-i-Kalim, Armughan-e-Hijaz

      Famous Punjabi Books/Poem: Piyaara Jedi, Baba Bakri Wala

      Allama Iqbal died at the age of 60

      Died: 21 April 1938

      Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

      Iqbal called himself:  Mureed e Hindi of Peer e Rumi


      ND14-7-2023

      79

      Who lead Muslim forces during the Crusades?

      Correct Answer: Salah-ud-din-aybbi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Crusades were a series of religious wars directed by the Latin Church in the medieval period period between 1095 and 1291

      **********

      Yusuf ibn Ayyub ibn Shadhi commonly known by the epithet Saladin

      An important figure of the Third Crusade,

      he spearheaded the Muslim military effort against the Crusader states in the Levant.

      He was the founder of the Ayyubid dynasty.

      Hailing from an ethnic Kurdish family,

      he was the first sultan of both Egypt and Syria.



      RK12022023
      80

      Harrapa is located in which division of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Sahiwal division
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The ruins of Harappa are situated in   Sahiwal District, Punjab, Pakistan

      Harappa, a village in eastern Punjab province, eastern Pakistan

      The Bronze Age Harappan civilization, now more often called the Indus Valley Civilisation

      Harappa is a small crossroads town of 15,000 people today.

      ND-5-09--023

      81

      Which party starts quit India movement?

      Correct Answer: Congress
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Know everything about Quit India Movement

      Started: Aug 8, 1942

      Started from: Bombay 

      Launched by: Gandhi

      Also known as: August Movement 

      Demand: to end British rule

      Jinnah changed the slogan "Divide and Quit India" 

      Started by party: Congress

      Quaid e Azam said about the Quit India movement an anti-Muslim action of congress

      82

      Quaid-e-Azam visited _________ times to Islamia College Peshawar.

      Correct Answer: Three
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Quaid-e-Azam visited 3 times to Islamia College Peshawar in:

      1936,

      1945 

      1948.

      Quaid-e-Azam who became a lifelong honorary member of the Khyber Union ICP in 1936 made the College one of the heirs of his property in 1939.

      ***

      Founded by Abdul Qayoom Khan in 1913.

      AGM 25-12-2022

      ND17-7-2023


      83

      When was Federal Board of Revenue (Erstwhile CBR) Founded?

      Correct Answer: 1st April, 1924
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      CBR stands for Central Board of Revenue.

      The Central Board of Revenue was created on April 01, 1924.

      through the enactment of the Central Board of Revenue Act, 1924

      In 1944, a full-fledged Revenue Division was created under the Ministry of Finance until 1960.

       In 1974, further changes were made to streamline the organization and its functions.

      In 1991, the CBR once again reverted to its original status under the Revenue Division,

      however, in January 1995, the Revenue Division was abolished and the CBR was once again reverted to a division of the Ministry of Finance.

      In 2007, the "FBR Act" was passed in July 2007,

      whereby the Central Board of Revenue was renamed to the present-day Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) and for a third time placed again under the Revenue Division of the Ministry of Finance

      *****

      Must Read it

      Who is the current Federal Minister for Investment Board__________?

      Who is the current chairman of FBR?

      Major languages of FATA are ___?

      Correct Answer: Pashto

      Buland Darwaza associated with which mosque?

      Correct Answer: Jamia Masjid
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      When did Zahir-ud-din Muhammad Babur was born?

      Show explanation

      Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodhi in first battle of Panipat in 1526.

      Babur is buried in Kabul.

      Wah city is famous for :

      90

      Distance from the state of Junagarh from Karachi?

      Correct Answer: 510 km
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The distance between Karachi and Junagadh is 511 kilometers (317 miles).
      • ***

      • The driving distance from Karachi to Junagadh is 7697 kilometers (4783 miles).
      91

      Which is the cultural capital of Pakistan ?

      Correct Answer: Lahore
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Lahore's culture is unique.

      It is sometimes referred as the cultural capital or the heart of Pakistan.

      The city was a seat of the Mughal Empire and the Sikh Empire as well as the capital of Punjab in Mahmud Ghaznavi's 11th century empire and in the British Empire.


      92

      The PAF started inducting female pilots in_________?

      Correct Answer: 2006
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Pakistan Air Force is the aerial warfare branch of the Pakistan Armed Forces, tasked primarily with the aerial defence of Pakistan, with a secondary role of providing air support to the Pakistan Army and Navy when required, and a tertiary role of providing strategic airlift capability to Pakistan.

      93

      The largest division of Sindh is:

      Correct Answer: Hyderabad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sindh is a province of Pakistan.

      Sindh is the third-largest province of Pakistan by land area

      Sindh is the second-largest province by population after Punjab

      Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam (the gateway of Islam),


      Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 district

      The mountain range located in Sindh is Kirthar Range

      There are three Barrages in Sindh

      The mountain range located in Sindh is Kirthar Range

      Sindhi is written in a version of the Arabic script.

      Sindhi is the official language of the Pakistani province of Sindh.

      The Sindhi language is the oldest language

      Karachi is the biggest Division of Sindh by population and Hyderabad is the largest Division of Sindh by area.

      What is the total estimated forest area of Sindh ?

      0.92 million hectares

      NAME OF 7 DIVISIONs OF SINDH:

      1. LARKANA DIVISION

      2. SUKKUR DIVISION

      3. SHAHEED BENAZIR ABAD DIVISION

      4. HYDERABAD DIVISION

      5. MIRPUR KHAS DIVISION

      6. KARACHI DIVISION

      7. BANBHORE DIVISION

      97

      How can the economy of Pakistan stabilize?

      Correct Answer: All of these
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      To reduce reliance on a few export sectors and increase resilience to global economic fluctuations,

      Pakistan should diversify its export base.

      Encouraging export-oriented industries, supporting small and medium enterprises (SMEs),

      and developing high-value-added products can help boost exports

      105

      Which country has sufi-culture and music?

      Correct Answer: Pakistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan: Sufi music is popular throughout the country,

      with famous Sufi shrines like those of Data Ganj Baksh in Lahore and Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai in Sindh attracting devotees and musicians.

      106

      Jahan Ara from Punjab founded the Punjab Provincial Muslim woman's league in

      Correct Answer: 1935
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In 1935, she founded the Punjab Provincial Women's Muslim League.

      In the Round Table Conference of 1930, she and Radhabai Subbarayan

      were the only two active members of women's organisations nominated to the conference;

      they argued unsuccessfully for a 5 per cent reservation for women in the legislatures.

      107

      Who led the Namaz e Janaza of Quaid E Azam?

      Correct Answer: Allama Shabbir Ahmed Usmani
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Quaid E Azam was the founder of Pakistan. 
      • Quaid E Azam died on 11 Sep, 1948 in Karachi. 
      • Allama Shabbir Ahmed Usmani Offered Funeral Prayer of Quaid E Azam at 3 p.m on Sunday, September, 11 in 1948. 
      • ****
      108

      Which document replaced the 1956 constitution of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Ayub Khan's Legal Framework Orders
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

      while

      Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

                  All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

                  1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

                  ***

                  Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

                  ***

                  In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

                  ****

                  40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

                  **

                  The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

                  **

                  The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

                  **

                  The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.

                  **

                  1956 constitution legislature was Unicameral

                  **

                  The Constitution of 1956 provided for a federal system with the principle of parity between East Pakistan and West Pakistan


                  109

                  In which year Public and Representative Office Disqualification Act 'PRODA' was promulgated?

                  Correct Answer: 1959
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • PRODA  was initially passed in 1949, was a notorious act
                  • In March 1959, the martial law administration of General Ayub Khan promulgated the Public Offices (Disqualification) Order patterned after the often misused PRODA
                  • PRODA: Public and Representative Office Disqualification Act
                  110

                  When the foundation of Islamia College Peshawar was laid?

                  Correct Answer: 1913
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Islamia College is a public university located in Peshawar.
                  • Founded by Abdul Qayoom Khan in 1913.
                  • Quaid-e-Azam visited 3 times to Islamia College Peshawar in:
                  • 1936
                  • 1945 
                  • 1948
                  111

                  When was the name of Lyallpur changed to Faisalabad?

                  Correct Answer: September 1, 1977
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The name of Lyallpur was changed to Faisalabad on September 1, 1977. 

                  Faisalabad, formerly known as Lyallpur, is the third-most-populous city in Pakistan.
                  Second-largest in the eastern province of Punjab.
                  *******
                  Pakistan City old name list
                  1-        Lyallpur was the old Name of Faisalabad
                  2-        Mahmood pur was the old Name of Lahore
                  3-        Kalanchi was the old Name of Karachi
                  4-        Raj Shahi was the old Name of Islamabad
                  5-        Persha pur was the old Name of Peshawar
                  6-        Khan Pur was the old Name of Gujranwala
                  7-        Cambell pur was the old Name of Attock
                  8-        Salwan Kot was the old Name of Sialkot
                  9-        Shal Kot was the old Name of Quetta
                  10-   Neroon Kot was the old Name of Hyderabad
                  11-   Khan Garh was the old Name of Jaccoabad
                  12-   Montgomery was the old Name of Sahiwal
                  13-   Pepri was the old Name of Bin Qasim
                  14-   Hindu Bagh was the old Name of Muslim Bagh
                  15-   Ajodhan pur was the old Name of Pakpatten
                  16-   Fort Sanemars was the old Name of Zhob
                  17-   owdayana was the old Name of Swat

                  112

                  Which chief of Istehkam-e-Pakistan party resigned in February 2024 from politics?

                  Correct Answer: Jahangir Khan Tareen
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Jahangir Tareen launches new political party Istehkam-i-Pakistan Party on 8 June 2023.

                  Tareen resigned on 12 February 2024.

                  *****

                  113

                  How many National Assembly of Pakistan seats will directly be elected in General Elections 2024?

                  Correct Answer: 266
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  128 million people registered to vote in the elections to pick 266 representatives in Pakistan. 

                  General elections will be held on February 8, 2024.


                  114

                  Syed Mohsin Naqvi became which number of chairman of Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB)?

                  Correct Answer: 37th
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Syed Mohsin Naqvi was elected unopposed as the 37th chairman of the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB)

                  Appointing date: 6 February, 2024

                  Tenue: three-year term

                  Replaced: Zaka Ashraf


                  115

                  Maria Toorpakai Wazir is the first tribal Pakistani international __________ player.

                  Correct Answer: Squash
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Maria Toorpakai Wazir became the first tribal Pakistani girl in international squash tournaments, turning professional in 2006.


                  116

                  The Durand Line is the 2640 km long border between Pakistan and ______

                  Correct Answer: Afghistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Durand Line is the 2,640-kilometer (1,640-mile).

                  Durand Line border between Afghanistan and Pakistan.

                  Old Length 2,611 km (1,622 mi)


                  History

                  Established 12 November 1893

                  Signing of the Durand Line Agreement at the end of the first phase of the Second Anglo-Afghan War


                  Current shape 8 August 1919

                  Anglo-Afghan Treaty of 1919 ratified at the end of the Third Anglo-Afghan War


                  117

                  Tashkent agreement is also called peace agreement, it was signed to formalize the ceasefire between Pakistan and _______ on January 10th, 1966.

                  Correct Answer: India
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Tashkent Declaration, an accord signed on January 10, 1966.

                  by India's Prime Minister Lal Bahadur Shastri (who died the next day) and Pakistan's President Mohammad Ayub Khan.

                  118

                  Lahore is in Punjab and Karachi is to ___

                  Correct Answer: Sindh
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Lahore is the capital of Punjab province 

                  Karachi is the capital of Sindh province 

                  119

                  Who was Abdullah Bin Ubey?

                  Correct Answer: see the answer in the detail
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Abd Allāh ibn Ubayy ibn Salul was a chieftain of the Khazraj tribe of Medina.

                  Upon the arrival of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, Ibn Ubayy seemingly became a Muslim


                  120

                  A “bale” is a standard size and weight of compressed

                  Correct Answer: Cotton
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  A Cotton bale is a standard sized and weighed pack of compressed cotton lint after grinning.

                  Different countries have their standards of bale size,

                  in Pakistan, the standard bale size is 170 kgs.

                  The cotton yield is calculated in terms of the number of bales.


                  121

                  Who is the first tribal woman to contest general elections 2024 from Bajaur district of KPK?

                  Correct Answer: Sultana Bibi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Sultana Bibi is the first tribal woman to contest general elections 2024 from Bajaur district of KPK.
                  • KPK: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                  • Note: General Elections for 16th National Assembly will be held on 8 February 2024.
                  122

                  Which Pakistani shooter has qualified for the Paris 2024 Olympics?

                  Correct Answer: Kishmala Talat
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • On 8 January 2024, Pakistani shooter Kishmala Talat qualified for the Paris 2024 Olympics after bagging a silver medal at the Asian Shooting Championship in Jakarta, Indonesia.
                  • Kishmala Talat is the first Pakistani female shooter to qualify for the Paris Olympics 2024.

                  123

                  Pakistani shooter Kishmala Talat won which medal in Asian Shooting Championship 2024?

                  Correct Answer: Silver medal
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • On 8 January 2024, Pakistani shooter Kishmala Talat qualified for the Paris 2024 Olympics after bagging a silver medal at the Asian Shooting Championship in Jakarta, Indonesia.

                  • Kishmala Talat is the first Pakistani female shooter to qualify for the Paris Olympics 2024.

                  124

                  Who has become first Pakistani female shooter to qualify for Paris Olympics 2024?

                  Correct Answer: Kishmala Talat
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Kishmala Talat, 21 is the first Pakistani female shooter to qualify for the Paris Olympics 2024.
                  • On 8 January 2024, Pakistani shooter Kishmala Talat qualified for the Paris 2024 Olympics after bagging a silver medal at the Asian Shooting Championship in Jakarta, Indonesia.
                  125

                  Under the 26th amendment, the seats of tribal districts in KP assembly has been increased from 16 to __________.

                  Correct Answer: 24
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  26th Amendment consists of

                  Increasing the National Assembly seats from 06 to 12

                  KPK provincial assembly from 16 to 24 of the erstwhile Federally Administered Tribal Areas

                  26th Amendments made on13 May 2019

                  ==========

                  25th Amendment consists of

                  Merges Federally Administered Tribal Areas with Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                  25th Amendment made in 31 May 2018

                  126

                  Who was the longest-serving Chief Justice of Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Mohammad Haleem
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Honourable Chief Justice Muhammad Haleem served as the Chief Justice of Pakistan from 1981 to 1989.

                  LENGTH OF TERMS IN DAYS: 3,205 days.

                  on 16 June 2019, an eminent poet won the Lifetime Achievement Award at the 8th UBL Literary Awards

                  Correct Answer: Zehra Nigah
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  on 16 June 2019, an eminent poet won the Lifetime Achievement Award at the 8th UBL Literary Awards.

                  Finally, Zehra Nigah was given the Lifetime Achievement Award by Simi Kamil.

                  128

                  On 15 May 2021, Justice Noor Meskanzai former Chief Justice of ___________ High Court took oath as Chief Justice Federal Shariat Court.

                  Correct Answer: Balochistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  He was appointed chief justice of the Islamic Court on 15 May 2021,

                  Justice Noor Meskanzai served as the 18th chief justice of Balochistan High Court from 26 December 2014 to 31 August 2018.

                  Meskanzai was fatally shot inside a mosque when he was performing ISHA prayer in (Masjid Ayesha) Balochistan's Kharan area, on 14 October 2022.

                  129

                  Risalo is the best known collection of romantic poetry in the ________ Language.

                  Correct Answer: Sindhi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai,  was a Sindhi Sufi mystic and poet from Pakistan

                  Greatest poet of the Sindhi language. 

                  Born: November 18, 1689, Hala

                  Died: December 21, 1752, Bhit Shah

                  Place of burial: Bhit Shah

                  Nationality: Indian, Pakistani

                  130

                  Shah Abdul Latif is the author of _____________ ?

                  Correct Answer: Shah jo Risalo
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Shah Abdul Latif Bhittai,  was a Sindhi Sufi mystic and poet from Pakistan

                  Greatest poet of the Sindhi language. 

                  Born: November 18, 1689, Hala

                  Died: December 21, 1752, Bhit Shah

                  Place of burial: Bhit Shah

                  Nationality: Indian, Pakistani

                  • Saveera Parkash is contesting the elections on a PPP ticket.

                  The Merger of NIB Bank into MCB Bank took place on_________?

                  Correct Answer: 7 JULY 2017
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  MCB Bank Limited is a Pakistani multinational commercial bank
                  Headquarters: Lahore
                  Founder: Adamjee Haji Dawood
                  Founded: July 9, 1947
                  *********
                  NIB Bank was a Pakistani bank which was based in Karachi, Pakistan.
                  Successor: MCB Bank
                  Founded: October 2003
                  Ceased operations: July 2017

                  145

                  Abaseen is another name of which river?

                  Correct Answer: Indus River
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Abaseen is another name of Indus River. 
                  • The Indus River starts in Tibet
                  • The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake
                  • The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,
                  • The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.
                  • River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta
                  • The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu
                  • National River of Pakistan: Indus River
                  • 6 Barrages constructed in Indus River
                  • Indus River Length: 3,180 km
                  • Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo
                  • The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 
                  • The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

                  146

                  What is the length of Islamabad-Peshawar Motorway?

                  Correct Answer: 155km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • M1 motorway connect Peshawar to Islamabad/Rawalpindi.
                  • M1 motorway was constructed by President Pervez Musharraf
                  • M1 motorway opened on October 2007.
                  • M1 motorway length 155 km, (88 km in KPK and 67 km in Punjab)
                  147

                  On which river will the Kalabagh Dam be constructed?

                  Correct Answer: Indus
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  • The                     Kalabagh Dam  is a proposed hydroelectric dam on the Indus River.
                  • The proposed site for the dam is situated at Kalabagh,
                  • in Mianwali district of Punjab. 
                  • Height: 83 m
                  • Location: Kalabagh, Mianwali District
                  • Active capacity: 7.52 km3 
                  • Installed capacity: 3,600 MW

                  148

                  Which Pakistan Air Force officer has recently secured prestigious Sword of Distinction in Australia?

                  Correct Answer: Muhammad Talha
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In December 2023, Officer Cadet Muhammad Talha of the Pakistan Air Force has been honored with the prestigious Sword of Distinction at the Australian Defence Force Academy.

                  149

                  Pakistan recorded its first death from monkeypox on which date?

                  Correct Answer: 19 December 2023
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  On 19 December 2023, The first death – due to Monkeypox – was reported in Pakistan, when a 40-year-old man undergoing treatment in Pakistan Institute of Medical Sciences (PIMS) Islamabad.

                  150

                  Name the first transgender person from KPK to contest general elections 2024?

                  Correct Answer: Sobia Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  A transgender person from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP) is aiming to contest in the upcoming polls, scheduled to be held on February 8, 2024.

                  Sobia Khan from Peshawar filed nomination papers for the provincial assembly seat from constituency PK-81.

                  ****

                  151

                  President Ayub Khan wrote a book called ___________?

                  Correct Answer: Friend not Masters
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  President Ayub Khan wrote a book called “Friends Not Master”

                  This book was published in 1967.


                  152

                  Sir Gamga Ram in whose name the Ganga Ram hospital exist in Lahore

                  Correct Answer: Engneer
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Sir Ganga Ram Hospital is a 550-bed hospital in Lahore, Pakistan.

                  Established by Sir Ganga Ram, a Civil Engineer

                  He established it in Lahore in 1921 during the British Raj


                  153

                  Sher Shah Suri ruled India from 1540 till which year?

                  Correct Answer: 1545
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Sher Shah Sur's real name is Farīd Khān.
                  • Sher Shah Suri is well-known for his Land Revenue System
                  • Sher Shah Suri Ruled India for 5 years from 1540 to 1545.
                  • Born: 1486, Sasaram, India
                  • Sher Shah Suri died: on 22 May 1545
                  • Sher Shah Suri introduced: Rupee Currency
                  • Sher Shah Suri built: GT Road
                  • Sher Shah took control of the Mughal Empire in 1540.
                  • Sher Shah Suri buried in Sasaram

                  154

                  Which province of Pakistan has conducted first ever artificial rain to ease smog?

                  Correct Answer: Pubjab
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Punjab government conducted first-ever successful experiment of artificial rain in Lahore with collaboration of United Arab Emirates (UAE).

                  Date: 16 December 2023

                  ****

                  155

                  Which river's annual flow is double then Nile river?

                  Correct Answer: Indus
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistani Indus River's annual flow is 58 cubic miles (243 cubic km) twice that of the Nile River

                  156

                  Ulema in Politics is written by?

                  Correct Answer: I.H Qureshi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Ishtiaq Husain Qureshi is the author of “Ulema in Politics"

                  Publication date: 1947 

                  It Is About The Role Played By The Ulema In The Politics Of Indian Sub-Continent And Covers The Period 1556-1947.

                  157

                  Exports during FY2019-20 reached _____ billion.

                  Correct Answer: US$ 21.39
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  During FY2019-20, the country's exports registered approximately a 6.81% decline,

                  falling from. $22.958 billion to $21.394 billion 

                  158

                  Fatima Jinnah contested elections against ______ in 1965.

                  Correct Answer: General Ayub Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  On 2 January 1965, Fatima Jinnah was a presidential candidate against Ayub Khan


                  There were four candidates: 

                  • Ayub Khan
                  • Fatima Jinnah
                  • and two obscure people with no party affiliation. 

                  Laltain was the election symbol of Fatima Jinnah 


                  Nominee
                  		Ayub Khan
                  		Fatima Jinnah
                  Party
                  		CML
                  		COP
                  Electoral vote
                  		49,951
                  		28,691
                  Percentage
                  		62.43%
                  		35.86%


                  Additional Information

                  Fatima Jinnah: younger sister of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah

                  She was born: 30th July 1893

                  She is referred to as Madr-e-Millat (Mother of Nation)

                  She died on 9 July 1967 at the age: of 71 in Karachi

                  Fatima Jinnah profession: Dental Surgeon

                  159

                  Who has become first woman Chief Traffic Officer (CTO) of Lahore?

                  Correct Answer: Amara Athar
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Punjab government appointed BS-19 police officer Amara Athar as chief traffic officer (CTO) of Lahore.
                  • Amara Athar is the first woman police officer holding the key traffic control assignment in Lahore.
                  • Appointed in December 2023.
                  • Amara Athar is also the fourth woman police officer posted in Lahore.
                  • **** 
                  160

                  The World's largest Dam is __________?

                  Correct Answer: Tarbela
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Tarbela Dam  is the largest earth-filled dam in the world

                  It is located in the Haripur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                  it is 485 feet (148 m) high above the Indus riverbed.

                  Its construction began in 1968

                  it was Completed in 1976

                  Construction was undertaken by the Italian firm Salini Impregilo.

                  161

                  'Muslims in India" book read by ____________?

                  Correct Answer: Abul Hasan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Muslims in India book written by Abul Hasan Ali Hasani Nadwi

                  It was published by Dar Ibn Kathir in 1953.

                  He was originally written in Arabic as Al Muslimun fil Hind. 

                  It's English translation was rendered by Mohammad Asif Kidwai.

                  162

                  Kerry-Lugar Bill for Pakistan was passed in __________?

                  Correct Answer: 2009
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The 'Enhanced Partnership with Pakistan Act of 2009

                  also known as the Kerry-Lugar-Berman Act

                  It was passed by the US Congress on 24 September and signed by President Obama on 15 October.

                  This bill provides Pakistan with a non-military aid package of US$1.5 billion annually, for the next five years

                  It was proposed by Senators John Kerry (D-Massachusetts) and Richard Lugar (R-Indiana).

                  163

                  Pakistan's 1st National Security Policy was approved in ___________?

                  Correct Answer: 2022
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  NSP stands for National Security Policy

                  The Public version of the NSP was launched on 14 January 2022 

                  NSP was approved by the federal cabinet on Dec 28, 2021.

                  Prime Minister Imran Khan reveals the country's first-ever NSP

                  The policy document is meant for a five-year period (2022-26)

                  164

                  Tirich Mir is the highest peak of _________ mountains.

                  Correct Answer: Hindu Kush
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Tirich Mir is the highest mountain in the Hindu Kush range

                  It’s the 33rd-highest peak in the world.

                  It is the highest mountain in the world

                  Tirich Mir is located in the Chitral District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

                  Tirich Mir height: 7708 meters 

                  Its first recorded climber on 21 July 1950 by a Norwegian


                  Additional Information:

                  Pakistan Highest Peaks List:

                  Himalayas range: Nanga Parbat

                  K-2: Krakarm range

                  Kirthar: Zardak peak

                  Koh e Suleman: Takhat e Sulaimani

                  Salt range: Sakesar

                  165

                  Why was the 1940 resolution presented?

                  Correct Answer: for greater Muslim autonomy
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  This resolution asked for greater Muslim autonomy of Muslim-majority states such as Punjab, Bengal, Sindh and NWFP within British India. 

                  On March 23, A.K. Fazul Haq, the Chief Minister of Bengal, moved the historical Lahore Resolution

                  ********

                  Lahore Resolution Translation English to Urdu: Mulana Zafar Ali Khan

                  Lahore Resolution Presented by A.K Fazul Haq        

                  Participate From Punjab (Leader): Zafar Ali Khan

                  Participate From Sindh (Leader): Sir Abdullah Haroon

                  Participate From Balochistan (Leader): Qazi Muhammad  Isa

                  Participate From KPK (Leader): Sardar Aurangzaib Khan

                  CM Banglad : A.K Fazul Haq 

                  United Province: Khalid Zaman

                  166

                  What was the purpose of forming Congress and Muslim League?

                  Correct Answer: To protect rights of the Hindus & Muslims
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The Muslim League was founded in 1906 in Dhaka
                  • The aim of representing the interests of Indian Muslims.
                  • AO Hume founded the Indian National Congress
                  • To form a platform for civil and political dialogue among educated Indians in 1885


                  Last updated by NA on 22-April-2026

                  167

                  When were the police reforms introduced in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: 2002
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The police reforms were introduced in Pakistan in 2002.

                  its name is Executive Order No. 22 of 2002

                  It replaced the Police Act of 1861 (V of 1861)


                  168

                  After India's independence Moulana Abdul Kalam Azam was appointed ?

                  Correct Answer: Minister of education
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  He served as First minister of education in the Indian government of Jawaharlal Nehru 

                  From 15 August 1947 – 2 February 1958

                  Prime Minister: Jawaharlal Nehru

                  Succeeded by: K.L. Shrimali

                  169

                  What is the old name of Hyderabad?

                  Correct Answer: Naroon Kot
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan City old name list

                  1-        Lyallpur was the old Name of Faisalabad

                  2-        Mahmood pur was the old Name of Lahore

                  3-        Kalanchi was the old Name of Karachi

                  4-        Raj Shahi was the old Name of Islamabad

                  5-        Persha pur was the old Name of Peshawar

                  6-        Khan Pur was the old Name of Gujranwala

                  7-        Cambell pur was the old Name of Attock

                  8-        Salwan Kot was the old Name of Sialkot

                  9-        Shal Kot was the old Name of Quetta

                  10-   Neroon Kot was the old Name of Hyderabad

                  11-   Khan Garh was the old Name of Jaccoabad

                  12-   Montgomery was the old Name of Sahiwal

                  13-   Pepri was the old Name of Bin Qasim

                  14-   Hindu Bagh was the old Name of Muslim Bagh

                  15-   Ajodhan pur was the old Name of Pakpatten

                  16-   Fort Sanemars was the old Name of Zhob

                  17-   owdayana was the old Name of Swat


                  170

                  What was the capital of King Dahir of Sindh?

                  Correct Answer: Arore
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Raja Dahir was the last Hindu ruler of Sindh in present-day Pakistan.

                  In 712 CE his kingdom was invaded by the Umayyad Caliphate led by Muhammad bin Qasim

                  where Dahir died while defending his kingdom.

                  Arore was the capital of King Dahir

                  ******

                  راجہ داہر موجودہ پاکستان میں سندھ کے آخری ہندو حکمران تھے۔ 712عیسوی میں اس کی سلطنت پر اموی خلافت نے حملہ کیا جس کی قیادت محمد بن قاسم کر رہے تھے۔

                  جہاں داہر اپنی سلطنت کا دفاع کرتے ہوئے مر گیا۔

                  171

                  Who are the closest allies of Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: All of these
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan has a long and strong relationship with China.

                  The long-standing ties between the two countries have been mutually beneficial.

                  A close identity of views and mutual interests remains the centre-point of bilateral ties.

                  172

                  Pakistan’s first-ever Anti-Rape Crisis Cell has been launched with the support of which country?

                  Correct Answer: USA
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In 2023, Pakistan’s “first-ever” anti-rape crisis cell (ARCC) was set up at Dr Ruth Pfau Civil Hospital Karachi to deal with a host of issues connected with sexual violence cases, their investigation and rehabilitation of rape survivors.

                  Supported by the USA

                   ****

                  173

                  Which city of Pakistan is famous for Handmade articles ( Handicraft)?

                  Correct Answer: Multan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Multan is one of the oldest regions in Pakistan where artisans create or decorate objects traditionally.

                  Additional Information

                  Khewra: Himalayan salt products

                  Sialkot: Clay pottery

                  Hyderabad: Wooden furniture, sports goods, and embroidery

                  Sillanwali: Wooden candy jars and other traditional containers


                  174

                  Who was the only five star General in Pakistan Army?

                  Correct Answer: General Ayub Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Ayub Khan also served in the civilian government as Defence and Home Minister from 1953 to 1958. He was the first and the only Field Marshall of the Pakistani Army, and assumed the Presidency after exiling President Iskandar Mirza in 1958.

                  175

                  Who is the first and only Pakistani women to summit Mount Everest?

                  Correct Answer: Samina Baig
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Samina Baig, a high-altitude female mountaineer from Gilgit-Baltistan, made history in 2013 when she became the first Pakistani woman to ascend Mount Everest

                  The highest official rank of Air Force is

                  Correct Answer: Air Chief Marshal

                  Explanation

                  Who is the current Chief Justice of Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Justice Qazi Faiz Isa

                  The headquarter of Special Security Unit (SSU), Sindh police is located in?

                  Correct Answer: Karachi

                  Headquarters:  Karachi, Sindh

                  In which year Pakistan tested its atom bombs?

                  Location: Chaghi, Balochistan

                  PM: Nawaz Sharif (2nd term)

                  In which year Pakistan hosted men’s ODI Cricket World Cup for the first time?

                  Edition: Fourth 

                  Format: One Day International (ODI)

                  Runners-up: England

                  Participating teams: 08

                  Total Matches: 27

                  About men’s Cricket World Cup 1987

                  Edition: Fourth

                  Venue: India & Pakistan

                  Winner: Australia (First title)

                  182

                  PCAA is a ____ organization.

                  Correct Answer: public
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan Civil Aviation Authority (CAA) is a public sector

                  It was Formed on 7 December 1982

                  They work under the Federal Government of Pakistan 

                  PCAA's head office is situated at Jinnah International Airport in Karachi.

                  PCAA is a member state of the International Civil Aviation Organization

                  44 civilian airports in Pakistan are owned 

                  183

                  Arif Ailvi is the _______ president of Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: 13
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Arif-ur-Rehman Alvi is a Pakistani politician currently serving as the 13th President of Pakistan,

                  He has served the Presidency since 9 September 2018.

                  Party:

                  • Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (1996–present)
                  • Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan (1979–1988)


                  He was Preceded by Mamnoon Hussain

                  He was a member of the National Assembly of Pakistan from June 2013 to September 2018

                  184

                  Which is not the district of Balochistan?

                  Correct Answer: Makran District
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Makran is a Division not District.

                  There are 36 Districts in Balochistan

                  ***

                  List of Balochistan Districts

                  Sr. No. District Area (km2) Population (2017)
                  1 Awaran 29,510 121,821
                  2 Barkhan 3,514 171,025
                  3 Kachhi 5,682 309,932
                  4 Chagai 44,748 226,517
                  5 Chaman 1,341 434,561
                  6 Dera Bugti 10,160 313,110
                  7 Duki 4,233 152,977
                  8 Gwadar 12,637 262,253
                  9 Harnai 2,492 97,052
                  10 Hub 6,716 339,640
                  11 Jafarabad 1,643 513,972
                  12 Jhal Magsi 3,615 148,900
                  13 Kalat 7,654 211,201
                  14 Kech 22,539 907,182
                  15 Kharan 14,958 162,766
                  16 Kohlu 7,610 213,933
                  17 Khuzdar 35,380 798,896
                  18 Lasbela 8,437 236,631
                  19 Loralai 3,785 244,446
                  20 Mastung 3,308 265,676
                  21 Musakhel 5,728 167,243
                  22 Nasirabad 3,387 487,847
                  23 Nushki 5,797 178,947
                  24 Qila Abdullah 3,553 323,793
                  25 Qila Saifullah 6,831 342,932
                  26 Panjgur 16,891 315,353
                  27 Pishin 6,218 736,903
                  28 Quetta 3,447 2,269,473
                  29 Sherani 4,310 152,952
                  30 Sibi 7,121 179,751
                  31 Sohbatpur 802 200,426
                  32 Surab 762 200,857
