                  185

                  Article 11 of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 deals with the?

                  Correct Answer: Child Labour and Slavery
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Article 11 of the Constitution adopted in 1973, and amended in 2015, includes the following provisions:

                  (1) Slavery is non-existent and forbidden and no law shall permit or facilitate its introduction into Pakistan in any form.

                  (2) All forms of forced labour and traffic in human beings are prohibited.

                  186

                  Where is the NADRA Headquarters located?

                  Correct Answer: Islamabad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  NADRA Headquarters is located in Islamabad.

                  NADRA stands for National Database and Registration Authority

                  NADRA Founded: March 10, 2000, Islamabad

                  The first CNIC started in 2000

                  NADRA Headquarters: Islamabad

                  NADRA Parent department: Ministry of Interior

                  The largest NADRA office is situated in Islamabad

                  ***

                  History

                  Pakistan initiated its National Identity Card System in 1973 under Article 30 of the Second Amendment.

                  The first NIC was that of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto, the Prime Minister of the time


                  187

                  MOI stand for _______

                  Correct Answer: Ministry of Interior
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  MOI stand for Ministry of Interior.

                  MOI is tasked and primarily responsible for:

                  • implementing the internal policies,
                  • state security,
                  • administration of internal affairs involving the state 
                  • assisting the government on territorial affairs
                  188

                  Pakistan’s first anti-rape crisis cell has been set up in which city?

                  Correct Answer: Karachi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In 2023, Pakistan’s “first-ever” anti-rape crisis cell (ARCC) was set up at Dr Ruth Pfau Civil Hospital Karachi to deal with a host of issues connected with sexual violence cases, their investigation and rehabilitation of rape survivors.

                   ****

                  189

                  The Makran Coastal Highway in Pakistan connects which two provinces?

                  Correct Answer: Sindh and Balochistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Makran Coastal Highway from Karachi in Sindh province to Gwadar in Balochistan 

                  This highway is also known as N-10 or National Highway 10

                  Highway Length is 653 km (406 mi)

                  The Construction started in 2002 

                  It was completed in 2004.

                  Highway covers Major cities:

                  • Karachi
                  • Ormara
                  • Pasni
                  • Gwadar



                  190

                  Which of the following is the most elevated (highest) pass in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Khunjerab Pass
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Khunjerab Pass is the highest mountain pass in Pakistan

                  It is located in Gilgit–Baltistan's Hunza and Nagar Districts.

                  The Other name: is Friendship Gate

                  Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 

                  Khunjerab Pass border connecting to China in the North of Pakistan

                  it is located in the Karakoram range.

                  191

                  The water from Jehlum, Chenab, Ravi, Sutlej, and Beas all come from?

                  Correct Answer: Punjnad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej are tributaries of the Indus River.

                  Panjnad means "five rivers"

                   above Mithankot, the Indus River receives from Panjnad 

                  Panjnad falls in River Indus

                  The length of Panjnad is: 71 km

                  The Panjnad Rive is located end of the Bahawalpur 

                   Panjnad Headworks has three canals:

                  1. Panjnad canal
                  2. Abbassia canal
                  3. Abbassia link canal


                  192

                  Which areas have been included in FATA by the 19th Constitution Amendment?

                  Correct Answer: Tribal Areas Adjoining Lakki Marwat and Tank Districts
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Tribal Areas, adjoining Laki Marwat and Tank districts, are declared to be part of FATA.

                  The Nineteenth Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan is a 2011 amendment expanding the FATA

                  FATA is 27220 square kilometers.

                  FATA consisted of two types of areas:

                  1.      Tribal Agencies (Tribal Districts)

                  2.      Frontier Regions (FRs).

                  There were seven Tribal Agencies and six Frontier Regions

                  History:

                  The proposed amendment was presented on 21 December 2010 by Chairman Raza Rabbani in the National Assembly

                   Amendment was Passed by the National Assembly on December 22, 2010,

                  by the Senate on December 30, 2010,

                  and assented to by the President on January 1, 2011

                  193

                  Most of the country's rivers flow into ___________?

                  Correct Answer: Indus River
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  the five rivers flow into the Indus

                  1. Jhelum
                  2. Chenab
                  3. Ravi
                  4. Beas,
                  5. Sutlej 

                  The Indus River starts in Tibet

                  The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

                  The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

                  The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

                  River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

                  The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

                  National River of Pakistan: Indus River

                  Indus River Length: 3,180 km

                  Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

                  The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

                  The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

                  194

                  Which is the largest District of FATA by population?

                  Correct Answer: Bajaur
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Bajaur agency is the largest District of FATA by population.

                  South Waziristan agency is the largest District of FATA by area.

                  195

                  When India annexed Junagadh?

                  Correct Answer: February 1948
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In February 1948, the princely state of Junagadh, located in what is now the Indian state of Gujarat,

                  was annexed to the Union of India after a dispute with the Dominion of Pakistan.



                  196

                  Which of the following book of Parveen Shakir?

                  Correct Answer: Khushbu
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Khusbhu is a volume of poetry written by Pakistani poet and columnist Parveen Shakir.

                  Published in 1976

                  Author: Parveen Shakir


                  197

                  When was Agra conference held?

                  Correct Answer: 14–16 July 2001
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Agra summit was a historic two-day summit meeting between India and Pakistan

                  which lasted from 14–16 July 2001.

                   It was held in the hopes of settling long-standing tensions between India and Pakistan.

                  198

                  What was the proportional share of Pakistan in the Assets?

                  Correct Answer: Rs. 750 Million
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  At the time, the Indian government had about Rs 400 crore in its kitty,

                  Division of assets finally took place in 4:1 ratio

                  with the Partition Council allotting the Pakistan Central Bank Rs 75 crore,

                  which included a working balance of Rs 20 crore made available to the Pakistanis in advance on August 15, 1947.

                  199

                  How much money Pakistan got at the time of partition?

                  Correct Answer: 75 crore
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  At the time, the Indian government had about Rs 400 crore in its kitty,

                  Division of assets finally took place in 4:1 ratio

                  with the Partition Council allotting the Pakistan Central Bank Rs 75 crore,

                  which included a working balance of Rs 20 crore made available to the Pakistanis in advance on August 15, 1947.


                  200

                  Basmati Paddy is harvested in Punjab in the month of :

                  Correct Answer: April
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The national rice crop is predominantly rainfed and it is planted from May-June during the kharif season.

                  The annual rice harvest generally occurs from October-December.


                  201

                  Lower Chenab Canal takes off from :

                  Correct Answer: Khanki Headworks
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                   The Lower Chenab Canal is a canal in Pakistan.

                  It was dug in 1892 and originates from Khanki Headworks,

                  which is situated on the River Chenab in Gujranwala District.

                  Some distributaries coming out of Lower Chenab Canal are the Jhang Branch, the Rakh Branch and the Gugera Branch Canal.

                  202

                  Who is the Administrative Head of province ?

                  Correct Answer: Chief Secretary
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Chief Secretary is the administrative head of a province in Pakistan.


                  203

                  The only player in cricket history who score century in ODI and test in his debut

                  Correct Answer: Abid Ali
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Abid Ali is a Pakistani international cricketer.

                  He made his List A debut in 2005 and first-class cricket debut in 2007.

                  He made his international debut for the Pakistan cricket team in March 2019. 

                  Born: October 16, 1987

                  204

                  The system of Diarchy remains operational during

                  Correct Answer: 1921-1937
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  diarchy particularly referred to the system of shared rule in British India established by the Government of India Acts 1919 

                  Dyarchy means the double system of government in which two heads are responsible for administration.

                  Dyarchy was introduced as a constitutional reform by Edwin Samuel Montagu and Lord Chelmsford 

                  The Government of India Act of 1935 ended the system of Diarchy and established 'provincial autonomy'.

                  205

                  What was the name of the English Company that ruled in the Indian Subcontinent?

                  Correct Answer: East Indian Company
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The East India Company was an English, and later British, joint-stock company

                  East India Company was established on December 31, 1600.

                  Ceased operations: June 1, 1874

                  Headquarters: London, United Kingdom

                  Founders: Thomas Smythe, Royal charter, John Watts

                  Defunct: 1 June 1874; 149 years ago


                  206

                  The Waziristan Hills are located in between which of the following rivers?

                  Correct Answer: Kurram and Gomal
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Waziristan lies between the Kurram River and the Gomal River.

                  The average height of the Waziristan hills is 1,500–2,500 meters (4,900–8,200 ft) above sea level.

                   Waziristan Hills is a mountainous region of northwest Pakistan near the Afghanistan border.

                  207

                  The Swat Valley attracts many domestic and foreign visitors, it is located in?

                  Correct Answer: Hindu Kush
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Swat is located in the Hindu Kush mountain range in KPK  

                  Swat is the 15th-largest district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

                  It is known as the Switzerland of Pakistan


                  KNOWN ABOUT SWAT

                  Swat District also known as the Swat Valley, is a district in the Malakand Division of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

                  Swat was merged with Pakistan in 1849

                  On 28 July 1969, General Yahya Khan announced the full integration states of Chitral, Dir, and Swat.

                  Capital: Saidu Sharif

                  Mingora is the largest city of Swat Valley

                  Gwalerai is a famous city for apples in Swat

                  ND26-9-2023

                  208

                  Name the Largest Tribal Agency in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: South Wazirstan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In Pakistan the largest tribal agency is South Waziristan.

                  South Waziristan District is a district in Dera Ismail Khan Division of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa 

                  It was known for being the largest of the former FATA agencies, covering an area of 6,620 sq km. 

                  209

                  Who is the current Director General (DG) of Pakistan Coast Guards (PCG)?

                  Correct Answer: Major General Jawad Riaz
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The current Director General (DG) of Pakistan Coast Guards (PCG) is Major General Jawad Riaz.

                  Last Update/verified 17.July.2025

                  SOURCE

                  OFFICIAL WEBSITE Click here
                  Wikipedia Click here


                  210

                  Which city of Pakistan is laid out on the shape of British flag?

                  Correct Answer: Faisalabad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Faisalabad Clock Tower is famous all over the world and is one of the oldest monuments.

                  It was constructed in a special way by placing the clock at the center of the eight markets which, from the top it looks like the Union Jack of the United Kingdom's flag.


                  211

                  Which important hills are located in Sindh?

                  Correct Answer: Kirthar
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Kirthar Mountains are a mountain range that marks the boundary between the Pakistani provinces of Balochistan and Sindh

                  Kirthar Mountains, a mountainous region in southern Pakistan.

                  It stretches about 300 km south from the Murat River in central-eastern Balochistan to Cape Muari (Monze), west of Karachi in the Arabian Sea.

                  The mountains form the boundary between the lower Indus River basin (east) 

                  The highest peak of the mountains is Zardak Peak at 7,430 ft (2,260 m).


                  212

                  Where is Malam Jabba resort located?

                  Correct Answer: Swat Valley
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Malam Jabba is a hill station and ski resort in the Hindu Kush mountain ranges.

                  Malam Jabba is nearly 40 km far from Saidu Sharif in the Swat Valley of KPK.

                   

                  213

                  When did Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto became the first civilian Martial Law Administrator?

                  Correct Answer: 20 December 1971
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                   Z A Bhutto was The First Civilian Chief Martial Law Administrator on 20 December 1971

                  Yahya Khan resigned on 20 December and transferred power to Bhutto,


                  • ****

                  Additional information 

                  • Iskandar Ali Mirza who was the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

                    Ø  Ayoub Khan was made chief martial law after this abrogation.

                    Ø  Feroz Khan Noun was the prime minister at the time of the first Martial Law in Pakistan.

                    ******

                    List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                    Sr#

                     Martial Law Number

                    Date of Martial Law

                    Martial Law Implemented by

                    1

                    First Martial Law

                    7th October 1958

                    Iskandar Mirza

                    2

                    2nd Martial Law

                    25th March 1969

                    General Yahya Khan

                    3

                    3rd Martial Law

                    25th July 1977

                    General Zia ul Haq

                    4

                    4th Martial Law

                    12th October 1999

                    General Pervaiz Musharraf

                     


                  214

                  Who gave this statement, "There should not be contradiction between the words of God and works of God"?

                  Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmad Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                   The statement "There should not be a contradiction between the words of God and the works of God" is indeed attributed to Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

                  He was a prominent 19th-century Muslim philosopher, reformer, and educationist in British India. 

                  215

                  What principle was supported by the Bengali politicians for the representation in the legislature and executive during the constitution making process?

                  Correct Answer: Population
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  During the constitution-making process in Pakistan, Bengali politicians from East Pakistan supported the principle of representation based on population.

                  216

                  On what ground the seats in the senate is allocated to the provinces?

                  Correct Answer: None of these
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In Pakistan, the seats in the Senate are allocated to the provinces based on equal representation

                  This structure is designed to give all provinces an equal voice in the federal legislative process.

                  217

                  What ceiling was fixed for the private ownership of irrigated land in the land reforms of 1959?

                  Correct Answer: None of these
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The 1959 Land Reforms Regulation of Pakistan limited the private ownership of irrigated land to 500 acres per individual: 


                  Irrigated: 500 acres per individual

                  Unirrigated: 1,000 acres per individual

                  218

                  How many areas is covered by Islamabad?

                  Correct Answer: 905 Sq km
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Islamabad covers an area of 906 square kilometers (350 sq mi).

                  Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967.


                  The area of Islamabad before its declaration as the capital of Pakistan was known as Raj Shahi.

                  Islamabad is the country's ninth-most populous city, with a population of over 1.2 million people

                  Constructed 1960

                  Postcode 44000

                  ND23-4-2023


                  219

                  What is the status of Council of Islamic Ideology?

                  Correct Answer: It is a constitutional body
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The Council of Islamic Ideology is a constitutional body of Pakistan, responsible for giving legal advice on Islamic issues to the government and the Parliament.
                  • This body was founded in 1962 under the government of Ayub Khan.
                  • The CII was established in 1962 under the government of Ayub Khan.
                  • The CII has a minimum of 9 and a maximum of 20 members.

                  220

                  On June 17 _____ assumed the charge of PTV as its chairman

                  Correct Answer: Arshad Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  on 17 Jun 2019, Arshad Khan assumed the charge of Pakistan Television Corporation (PTV) as its chairman

                  He was elected by the PTV’s Board of Directors 

                  ****

                  On November 26, 1964, the first official television station commenced transmission broadcasts from Lahore, followed by Dhaka in 1965 (then the capital of East Pakistan).

                  A third center was established in Rawalpindi-Islamabad in 1965 and the fourth in Karachi in 1966.

                  Pakistan Television (PTV) started its live transmission on November 26, 1964

                  ****

                  Who started the Nationalization of Banks in Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: Z.A. Bhutto
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Z.A. Bhutto started the Nationalization of Banks in Pakistan in 1974.

                  Banks (Nationalization) Act, 1974

                  This act was passed by Parliament on January 23, 1974.

                  It nationalized 14 banks and put them under the direct control of the Government of Pakistan.

                  Born: 21 September 1919 in Hazara District

                  Died: 26 July 1988 (aged 68) Chicago, Illinois, United States

                  In which war Ahmad Khan Kharl fought against the British in Punjab?

                  Correct Answer: None of these
                  Show explanation

                  In the Bar region of Pakistani Punjab.

                  He was a Punjabi Muslim chieftain and leader of the Kharal tribe.

                  He died fighting the British on September 21, 1857, at the age of 81

                  Whose behavior dishearten M.A. Jinnah and decided to stay away from India?

                  227

                  When was the separate electorate demanded for the first time by the Muslims in British India?

                  Correct Answer: During Simla deputation
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In 1906, Simla Deputation the separate electorate demanded for the first time by the Muslims in British India

                  All important Question Related to " Separate Electorate" 

                  Indian Councils Act of 1909, also called Morley-Minto Reforms

                  Minto-Morley Reforms Muslim's demand for a separate electorate was incorporated

                  In 12 March 1909 the Morley-Minto Changes allowed partitioned electorates to Muslims.

                  This Act further extended democratic participation by increasing the size of the legislative councils, both Central and provincial.

                  27 out of add up to 60 members were to be chosen and 5 seats were saved for the Muslims.

                  Indian Council Act 1909 separate electorate for Muslims was accepted

                  on 28 August 1928 Nehru Report rejected the demand for a separate electorate

                  Congress and other Hindu bodies opposed the separate electorate

                  240

                  The area of Wakhan is under the control of ________.

                  Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The area of Wakhan is, under the control, of Afghanistan. 

                  The Wakhan Corridor is a narrow strip of territory in Afghanistan, extending to China and separating Tajikistan from Pakistan and Kashmir.

                   The corridor was formed in 1893 and created the Durand Line.

                  The Wakhan Corridor is about 350 km long

                  241

                  Karachi steal mills were established in _______?

                  Correct Answer: 1968
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Karachi Steel Mill was established on the 2nd of July, 1968, and the government decided to sponsor a state-owned Karachi steel mill.

                  It was a separate corporation according to the Act of 1913.

                  while

                  The Pakistan Steel Mills Corporation colloquially referred to as Pak Steels

                  Predecessor: Karachi Steel Projects

                  Founded: 30 December 1973 (49 years ago)

                  Headquarters: Karachi

                  It was founded on 2nd July 1973 by Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto.

                  The completion of the Steel Mill was launched by General Zia Ul Haq on 15 January 1985.

                  Steel Mill was established with the support of Russia.

                  On May 12 Pakistan and IMF reached a staff-level agreement for a _________ bailout over year

                  (IMF) approved a 9-month (SBA) for Pakistan for an amount of SDR2,250 million (111 percent of quota) 

                  Ernesto Ramirez Rigo visited Islamabad, Pakistan from April 29 to May 11, to discuss IMF support for the authorities' economic reform program.

                  244

                  What is Sir Creek line also known as?

                  Correct Answer: Ban Ganga
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Sir Creek opens up into the Arabian Sea.

                  It was originally named Ban Ganga and was named Sir Creek during colonial times after a British official.

                  Sir Creek is a 96-km long water strip in the marshlands of Rann of Kutch.

                  Sir Creek tidal estuary in the uninhabited marshlands of the Indus River Delta on the border between India and Pakistan.

                  The creek flows into the Arabian Sea and separates Gujarat state in India from Sindh province in Pakistan.

                  Badshahi Mosque was built by Emperor Aurangzeb in 1671,

                  Badshahi Mosque Height: 69 m

                  Number of minarets in Badshahi Mosque: 8

                  Sargodha is famous for?

                  Correct Answer: organies
                  Show explanation

                  Sargodha is Pakistan's 12th-largest city by population

                  Sargodha is also known as the City of Eagles

                  Sargodha was founded by the British as a canal colony in 1903

                  Pakistan City is famous for:

                  • Lahore is famous for its food, qawali music
                  • Sialkot is known for manufacturing and export of many items
                  • Gujrat is famous for peanuts, sugar cane, cotton, milk, and milk products.
                  • Rawalpindi is also famous for its Historical Places. One of them is Purana Qila
                  247

                  Lord Wavell offered a scheme for the settlement of the future political problem of India is known as _______

                  Correct Answer: Wavell plan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Lord Wavell, the Governor-General of India,  resolving the deadlock in Indian politics, put forward a plan which is known as the 'Wavell Plan' announced in 1945.

                  The Wavell Plan was first presented at the Shimla Conference in 1945.

                  The Wavell Plan aimed to address the growing demand for Indian independence and provide a roadmap for constitutional progress.

                  Lord Wavell announced all members except the Viceroy and the Commander in Chief would be Indians.

                  ND15-10-2023


                  248

                  When Raja Pervaiz Ashraf become the Prime Minister of Pakistan?

                  Correct Answer: 2012
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Raja Pervaiz Ashraf  served as the 19th prime minister of Pakistan

                  He worked as PM from 22 June 2012 until completing his designated term on 16 March 2013.

                  He elects the 21st Speaker of the National Assembly of Pakistan in 2022.

                  He belongs to the Pakistan People's Party (PPP).

                  249

                  On which date East Bengal Legislative Assembly election 1954 was held?

                  Correct Answer: between 8 and 12 March 1954
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Legislative elections were held in East Bengal between 8 and 12 March 1954,

                  the first since Pakistan became an independent country in 1947.

                  The opposition United Front led by the Awami League and Krishak Sramik Party won a landslide victory with 223 of the 309 seats.

                  The Muslim League Chief Minister of East Pakistan Nurul Amin was defeated in his own constituency by Khaleque Nawaz Khan by over 7,000 votes,

                  all the Muslim League ministers lost their seats.

                  The United Front cabinet was formed on 15 May 1954 under the leadership of Sher-e-Bangla A. K. Fazlul Huq (CM). 




                  250

                  Poona Pact, 1932, was signed between ______.

                  Correct Answer: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Mahatma Gandhi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Poona Pact was signed by Madan Mohan Malviya 

                  On behalf of Mahatma Gandhi and B.R. Ambedkar 

                  At Yerwada Central Jail in Pune on September 24, 1932.

                  It was signed during the time of Lord Willington.


                  251

                  Who dissolved the National Assembly in 1990?

                  Correct Answer: Ghulam Ishaq Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In The General elections of the eighth National Assembly on 16th November 1988

                  The Assembly was dissolved by the President, Ghulam Ishaq Khan under Article 58(2)(b) on 6th August 1990.

                  while 

                  The Assembly was dissolved by President Farooq Ahmad Khan Laghari on 5th November 1996.

                  252

                  The process of separation of East Pakistan was secretly supported by _________

                  Correct Answer: America
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The process of separation of East Pakistan was secretly supported by America.

                  This sets up a framework for blaming Pakistan’s current problems with the United States as well

                  In 1971, an internal crisis in Pakistan resulted in a third war between India and Pakistan and the secession of East Pakistan, creating the independent state of Bangladesh.

                  These events altered the relationship between the United States and the region.

                  253

                  Who introduced the Shariat Bill?

                  Correct Answer: Nawaz Sharif
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  in 1998 Sharif in his first term introduced the “Enforcement of Shariat Bill” in the two houses

                  The Senate (upper house of parliament) adopted the "Shariat Bill" on May 28 and paved the way to impose Islamic law throughout Pakistan. 

                  Sharif introduced the Shariat bill in the National Assembly to make the Quran and Sunnah the supreme laws in Pakistan.


                  The Ninth Amendment to the Constitution of Pakistan is related to the Sharia Bill


                  254

                  On August 21 2002 Musharraf issued the____________.

                  Correct Answer: LFO
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Legal Framework Order, 2002 was issued by Pakistani President Pervez Musharraf in August 2002.

                  provided for the general elections of 2002 and

                  the revival of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan, and added numerous amendments to the Constitution.

                  It targeted the law-making bodies of Pakistan and empowered him to dismiss the national and provincial assemblies in Pakistan.

                  As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

                   As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

                  Zia-Ul-Haq Died on 17-08-1988 by Plane crashed in Bahawalpur

                  Born: August 12, 1924, Jalandhar, India

                  Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq becomes 6th President of Pakistan.

                  General Zia Ul Haq ruled Pakistan from 1977 to 1988

                  The provincial constitution order martial law in 1977.

                  When General Zia Ul Haq addressed in UN? 23 Oct 1985

                  He remains the country's longest-serving de facto head of state and Chief of Army Staff

                  Ghulam Ishaq Khan succeeded Zia Ul Haque as President of Pakistan.

                  The road connecting Pakistan and China is called Karakoram Highway

                  Khunjerab pass connects Pakistan with China

                  The Karakoram Highway (often abbreviated to KKH)

                  The Karakoram Highway, also known as the Friendship Highway in China, was built by the governments of Pakistan and China

                  Karakoram highway is also known as N-35

                  The Karakoram Highway starts from Hassan Abdal.

                  261

                  What are the main points of the Indus Water Treaty?

                  Correct Answer: water dispute
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Indus Waters Treaty was signed in 1960 after nine years of negotiations between India and Pakistan with the help of the World Bank

                  ****

                  The Treaty allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan

                  and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India

                  Indus Waters Treaty, treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan

                  Indus Water Treaty between India and Pakistan was brokered by World Bank


                  ***

                  The Treaty was signed at Karachi by Field Marshal Mohammad Ayub Khan,

                  the then President of Pakistan, Jawaharlal Nehru 

                  the then Indian Prime Minister, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif 

                  The Treaty is effective from 1st April 1960

                  263

                  Parliament has __________ House in 1956 constitution.

                  Correct Answer: 1
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Under the 1956 Constitution, Parliament was unicameral (one House).

                  Legislative powers vested in the Parliament,

                  which consisted of the President and the National Assembly comprising 300 Members

                  divided equally between East and West Pakistan.

                  The cabinet was presided over by the Prime Minister.

                  The Constitution declared that there would be only one house of parliament known as the National Assembly.

                  ***

                  History
                  Parliament has __01__ House in the 1956 constitution.
                  Parliament has __01__ House in 1962 constitution.

                  Parliament has __02__ House in 1973 constitution.

                  264

                  The Pakistan's Supreme Judicial Council is comprised of ______?

                  Correct Answer: 5 members
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Supreme Judicial Council is comprised of 5 members:

                  • Chief Justice of Pakistan.
                  • Two next most Senior judges of the supreme court.
                  • Two most Senior Chief Justices of High Court.

                  ****

                  The Supreme Judicial Council of Pakistan is a body of judges empowered under Article 209 of the constitution of Pakistan to hear cases of misconduct against judges.

                  265

                  The Non-cooperation Movement started in ________.

                  Correct Answer: 1920
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Non-Cooperation Movement started in 1920.

                  The leader of the Non-cooperation Movement was Mahatma Gandhia and Muhammad Ali Jinnah

                  The non-cooperation movement was a mass movement that involved participation from the nationalists as well as the public. 

                  After a series of events including the Jallianwala Bagh Massacre in 1920.

                  The Non-cooperation Movement was called off by Mahatma Gandhi after the Chauri Chaura incident in February 1922. 

                  RK81023

                  266

                  The last great Mughal emperor Aurangzeb Alamgir last resting place is in

                  Correct Answer: Khuldabad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Tomb of Aurangzeb, the last influential Mughal emperor, is located in Khuldabad, Aurangabad district, Maharashtra, India.

                  Aurangzeb died in 1707 at Ahmednagar.

                  His body was then carried to Khuldabad after his son Azam Shah and his daughter Zeenat-un-Nissa arrived at their father's camp.

                  There is a platform over the tomb made of red stone, less than three yards in length.


                  267

                  Who led the foundation of the Sayyid dynasty in India ?

                  Correct Answer: Khizer Khan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Sayyid Dynasty was founded by Khizr Khan, who was the governor of Multan and Timur's deputy in India.

                  It was the fourth dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate who ruled from 1414 to 1451.

                  Khizr Khan captured Delhi from Daulat Khan Lodi on May 28, 1414 thereby establishing the Sayyid dynasty.

                  268

                  Syed Mehdi Ali was the real name of __________?

                  Correct Answer: Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk
                  Show explanation

                  Other names: Syed Mehdi Ali, Munir Nawaz Jang

                  He was involved in the Aligarh Movement and was one of the founders of the All-India Muslim League in 1906.

                  Born: December 9, 1837, Etawah, India

                  Died: October 16, 1907, Shimla, India

                  Party: All-India Muslim League

                  Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan

                  Born: 1177, Marand, Iran

                  Died: February 19, 1274, Sehwān

                  Place of burial: Darbar Hazrat Usman Marvandi Lal Shahbaz Qalandar, Sehwān

                  Title: Qalandar

                  The shrine of Hazrat Laal Shahbaz Qalandar is situated in Sehwan Sindh

                  Correct Answer: Liaqat Ali Khan

                  who served as the first prime minister of Pakistan from 1947 until his assassination in 1951.

                  273

                  Pakistan planning commission was established in

                  Correct Answer: 1952
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Planning Commission is a financial and public policy development institution of the Government of Pakistan.

                  The Commission comes under Ministry of Planning, Development and Reforms.

                  The Planning Commission undertakes research studies and state policy development initiatives for the growth of national economy.

                   Expansion of the public and state infrastructure of the country in tandem with the Ministry of Finance.

                  274

                  Statue of animals was discovered at Harrapa

                  Correct Answer: Bull
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The most common animal figure found at all the Harappan sites is unihorn bull.

                  Harappa was discovered in 1921 by Dayaram Sahni.

                  The excavations were done under the guidance of Sir John Marshal and Colonel Meke.

                  Remains of the Harappan civilisation has been found in other cities like Mohen-Jo-Daro, Ropar, and Alamgirpur.


                  275

                  When Muslim League demand for principal of self-rule for India?

                  Correct Answer: 1913
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                   In 1913, the Muslim League adopted the principle of self-rule.

                  This brought Congress and Muslim League closer to each other. 

                  The league wanted a separate nation for India's Muslims because it feared that an independent India would be dominated by Hindus.


                  276

                  On 14 August 1947 the only fully operational Muslim Bank in Pakistan's territory was :

                  Correct Answer: Allied Bank Ltd
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Allied Bank (ABL)(الائیڈ بینک لمیٹڈ) is the fifth largest commercial bank in Pakistan .

                  Allied Bank with its registered office in Lahore.

                  It was the first Muslim bank established before Pakistan became independence in 1942 with the name of Australasia Bank.

                  It was renamed to Allied Bank of Pakistan in 1974.


                  277

                  The tributaries of Indus river coverage to their confluence with the Indus at :

                  Correct Answer: Mithankot
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Indus Sources: Tibetan Plateau, Senge Zangbu, Gar River

                  The Indus Tributaries:

                    • Sutlej River
                    • Chenab River
                    • Jhelum River
                    • Beas
                    • Ravi River

                    ****

                    The Indus River starts in Tibet

                    The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

                    The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

                    The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

                    River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

                    The old name of the Indus River was: Sindhu

                    National River of Pakistan: Indus River

                    6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

                    Indus River Length: 3,180 km

                    The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot 

                    The upper Indus Plain starts from Kalabagh

                    278

                    Who lead the foundation of British India association

                    Correct Answer: Dadabhai Naoroji
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The British India Association was indeed founded by Dadabhai Naoroji in 1866.

                    Dadabhai Naoroji, often referred to as the "Grand Old Man of India,"

                    Dadabhai Naoroji was born on September 4, 1825, in Bombay (now Mumbai), British India.

                    he foundation of the British India Association marked an important milestone in his efforts to address the concerns of Indians under British rule.

                    The association aimed to provide a platform for Indians to voice their grievances, discuss political issues, and work towards the betterment of Indian society.


                    279

                    Forests in Pakistan are ___ % of its total territorial area .

                    Correct Answer: 5.2
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan covers an area of 87.98 million ha;

                    this includes 4.57 million ha of forests,

                    constituting 5.2 percent of the total land area

                    280

                    What is the maximum water storage level of Mangla dam ?

                    Correct Answer: 1242 feet
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    he Mangla Dam is situated on the Jhelum River 

                    It is the seventh-largest dam in the world.

                    Mangla Dam constructed on River Jhelum is to the help of the World Bank is located in Kashmir

                    Mangra Dam is a multipurpose dam on the  Jhelum River in the Milpool district of Pakistan.

                    Mangra Dam is named after the nearby village of Mangra.

                    Construction was started in 1961 and it was completed in 1967.

                    Mangla Dam has 5 tunnels and a power station

                    Mangra Dam is a multipurpose dam on the  Jhelum River in the Milpool district of Pakistan.

                    Mangra Dam is named after the nearby village of Mangra.

                    Construction was started in 1961 and it was completed in 1967.

                    Mangla Dam has 5 tunnels and a power station

                    282

                    To which dynasty did Razia Sultan, the first and last female ruler of Delhi Sultanate belong _____

                    Correct Answer: Mamluk dynasty
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Mamluk dynasty was a dynasty established in Northern India by Qutb ud-Din Aibak.

                    Razia Sultana was the daughter of Iltumish,

                    the third Muslim Turkic sultan of the Sultanate of Delhi.

                    Razia was the first and last Muslim lady to rule Delhi.


                    283

                    What Is Cash Crop?

                    Correct Answer: A crop that is not a food crop; it is grown for sale to manufactures who convert it into products.
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sugarcane, rice, wheat and cotton are the cash crop of Pakistan.


                    284

                    What Is Cash Crop ?

                    Correct Answer: A crop that is not a food crop; it is grown for sale to manufactures who convert it into products.
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sugarcane, rice, wheat and cotton are the cash crop of Pakistan.

                    A cash crop, also called profit crop, is an agricultural crop which is grown to sell for profit.

                    It is typically purchased by parties separate from a farm.


                    285

                    Article 31 of the constitution of 1973 lays down that the state shall endeavor with regard to the Muslims of Pakistan to

                    Correct Answer: Secure the proper organization of Zakat and Usher
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                        Article 31 of the constitution of 1973 lays down

                    that the state shall endeavor with regard to the Muslims of Pakistan to Secure the proper organization of Zakat and Usher

                    286

                    Khar is the main town of :::

                    Correct Answer: Bajaur agency
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Khar, is the headquarters of Bajaur District in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.

                    Khar was one of the largest towns in the Federally Administered Tribal Areas.

                    It lies near the border with Afghanistan.

                    287

                    National Desert of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Thar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The National Desert of Pakistan is the Thar desert

                    It is the 16th largest desert on the planet and the third largest in Asia.

                    15% located in Pakistan

                    85% located in India

                    Also called Friendly Desert

                    Located near Badin, Sindh 

                    Famous for: Sand 

                    Coal also found in Thar 

                    also known as Great Indian Desert

                    7th largest desert on the planet

                    3rd largest in Asia.

                    Area: 77,000 Miles

                    ****

                    Pakistan hosts five major deserts which were historic forests.

                    1.       Cholistan (Bahalpur, Punjab)

                    2.       Thal (Bkhar district of Punjab)

                    3.       Katpana desert (Sakardu)

                    4.       Kharan desert (Balochistan)

                    5.       Thar desert (Sindh)

                    In KPK there is no desert

                    The northern area of Pakistan is known as the “Deserts Free Area”


                    288

                    Benazir Bhutto buried in________?

                    Correct Answer: Garhi Khuda Bakhsh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Bhutto family mausoleum is situated at Garhi Khuda Bakhsh, in Larkana District, Sindh, Pakistan.

                    The Mazar contains the graves of the Bhutto family,

                    and is the burial place of Zulfikar, Murtaza, Shahnawaz, Nusrat, and Benazir Bhutto


                    BB was Born on June 21, 1953, in Karachi

                    BB was Assassinated on December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi

                    BB Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007)

                    BB Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

                    BB Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977),

                    BB Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.

                    Prime Minister

                    She was sworn in as the Prime Minister of Pakistan on 2 December 1988.

                    As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

                     As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

                    Farooq Laghari dissolved Benazir Bhutto's second government.

                    She was the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                    education :

                    Which Nursery School did Benazir Bhutto attend? Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.

                    Which college did Benazir Bhutto attend? Radcliffe College

                    Which University did Benazir Bhutto attend? University of Oxford,

                    292

                    The third constitution of Pakistan was enforced in the year

                    Correct Answer: 1973
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The National Assembly approved the 1973 Constitution on April 10, 1973,

                    and it came into effect on August 14, 1973.

                    Shaheed Zulfikar Ali Bhutto took over as the Prime Minister of Pakistan from this date

                    and Fazal Ilahi Chaudhry was appointed as the President of Pakistan.

                    This Constitution is called the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973

                    and was promulgated on 14th August 1973.

                    ****

                    1. The constitution of 1956 was abrogated by Iskander Mirza (7 October 1958)

                    2. The Constitution of 1962 was abrogated by Yahya Khan (26th March 1969)

                    3.  Constitution of 1973 (Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto)

                    293

                    The national mountain of Pakistan is?

                    Correct Answer: K-2
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin

                    K2 mountain was first measured by Godwin Austen in 1856.

                    Godwin Austen was an English topographer (surveyor) 

                    'K' standing for Karakoram.

                    Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.

                     after the world's highest peak Mount Everest.

                    Mount K2 is part of the Karakorum Range located on the border of Pakistan and China.

                    K2 height 8,611 meters

                    It is located on the China–Pakistan border between Baltistan in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of northern Pakistan.

                    294

                    What was the national airline of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: PIA
                    Show explanation

                    Headquarters: Karachi

                    Founded: October 29, 1946

                    Founder: Mirza Ahmad Ispahani

                    Orient Airways was the old name of PIA

                    Orient Airways Ltd. was an airline established in 1946 new name as Pakistan International Airlines in 1955.

                    PIA flight landed in China in 1963

                     PIA, in 1964 became the first non-Communist airline to fly to China

                    Length of Warsak Dam?

                    Length: 140.2 m (460 ft)

                    Warsak Dam is built on the Kabul river

                    Construction began 1949

                    Opened: 1960

                    Warsak Dam was completed under the Colombo Plan in two phases and financed by the Canadian Government.

                    When Badr-1 was launched?

                    Correct Answer: 1990

                    Badr-A Launch mass: 52 kilograms (115 lb)
                    Badr-A Last contact: 21 August 1990

                    Sadh Belo is located in?

                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    298

                    After the merge in KPK former areas of FATA will have ___ is KPK assembly?

                    Correct Answer: 21
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    FATA will have 21 seats within the KP Assembly:

                    • 16 general
                    • 4 reserved for women
                    • 1 reserved for non-Muslims

                    The incumbent senators for FATA will be allowed to complete their six-year terms.

                    The Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly will have 145 seats

                    • 115 general
                    • 26 reserved for women
                    • 4 reserved for minorities

                    FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018  under the 25th Amendment.

                    Gilgit-Baltistan, formerly known as the Northern Areas, is a region administered by Pakistan as an administrative territory.

                    Gilgit-Baltistan was Established on 1 November 1948

                    Capital of Gilgit-Baltistan is Gilgit

                    Largest city of Gilgit-Baltistan is Skardu

                    Who has become first ever woman registrar of Supreme Court of Pakistan?

                    310

                    Who is the constitutional head of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: President of the country
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    CHAPTER 1 - THE PRESIDENT

                    41. The President.

                    (1) There shall be a President of Pakistan who shall be the Head of State and shall represent the unity of the Republic.

                    (2) A person shall not be qualified for election as President unless he is a Muslim of not less than forty-five years of age and is qualified to be elected as member of the National Assembly.

                    (3) The President to be elected after the expiration of the term specified in clause ( 7) shall be elected in accordance with the provisions of the Second Schedule by the members of an electoral college consisting of:

                    (a) the members of both Houses; and

                    (b) the members of the Provincial Assemblies.

                    How much prize money on national anthem of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: 10000 Rupees
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    About National Anthem

                    The prize money on national anthem of Pakistan is 10000 Rupees first time

                    National Anthem was written by Hafeez Jalandhari in 1952 

                    the music was produced by Ahmad G. Chagla in 1949.

                    It was officially adopted as Pakistan's national anthem on 13 August 1954.

                    Ahmed Rushdi recorded the National Anthem of Pakistan in 1954.

                    After 68 years Pakistan's National Anthem recorded

                    15 lines in the National Anthem of Pakistan


                    There are 21 musical instruments were used in National Anthem

                    National Anthem was sung by 11 singer

                    48 Words in the National Anthem of Pakistan

                    The National Anthem of Pakistan was played for the first time in front of Raza Shah Rahalvi of Iran

                    In the National Anthem of Pakistan, " Jan-i-Istaqbal" stands for the Symbol of ambitions.

                    the first national anthem of Pakistan was played on Pakistan's national radio on 14 August 1947

                    80 sec is the duration of the National Anthem of Pakistan

                    Explanation
                    • The book Bang-i-Dara (The Call of the Marching Bell)
                    • A philosophical poetry book by Muhammad Iqbal.
                    • Published in Urdu in 1924,
                    • It is his first collection of Urdu poetry


                    Last updated on 09-03-2026

                     

                    324

                    Who has become the first woman SSP of Balochistan province?

                    Correct Answer: Faryal Fareed
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    For the first time in Balochistan's history, a female police officer took charge as the senior superintendent of police (SSP) in the Jaffarabad district of the province.

                    Hailing from Haripur, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KP), Faryal Fareed was appointed as the Jaffarabad SSP in September 2023.


                    ****

                     

                    325

                    What is the abbreviation of NDLP ?

                    Correct Answer: National Digital Library Program

                    326

                    Taxila was the capital of_____?

                    Correct Answer: Ghandhara
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Taxila was the capital of Ghandhara

                    Gandhara was an ancient kingdom situated along the Kabul and Swat rivers of Afghanistan and Pakistan. 

                     Taxila was designated a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1980.

                    The old name of Taxila wasTakhshasila

                    Taxila's Province in Punjab

                    Taxila's Division is Rawalpindi Division

                    Taxila's District is Rawalpindi District

                    Taxila's Tehsil is Taxila Tehsil

                    Old Taxila was an important city in ancient India, 

                    Taxila is one of Punjab's popular tourist spots

                    327

                    On which mountain range is Islamabad located?

                    The Margalla Hills within the Margalla Hills National Park 

                    The Margalla range has an area of 12,605 hectares.

                    The highest peak of Margalla Hills is Tilla Charouni

                    Elevation: 1,604 m (5,262 ft)

                    Khanjrab is a ___

                    How many ordnance factories were working in the united subcontinent till 1947

                    Show explanation

                    After the creation of Pakistan in 1947, all those sixteen factories fell to Indian share since none of them was located in the areas forming Pakistan.

                    338

                    Who Was The First To Raise The Slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad'?

                    Correct Answer: Maulana Hasrat Mohani
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    slogan was coined by the Urdu poet, Indian freedom fighter, and leader of the Indian National Congress Maulana Hasrat Mohani in 1921.

                    It was popularized by Bhagat Singh during the late 1920s 

                    Inquilab Zindabad is an Urdu phrase, which translates to "Long live the revolution". It was first used by Muhammad Iqbal.

                     It was also the official slogan of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association

                    Which mountain separates Central Asia and South Asia?

                    Area: 595,000 km²

                    When the first battle for the freedom of Jammu and Kashmir was fought?

                    Correct Answer: 1931
                    Show explanation

                    All India Muslim League Headquarters: Lucknow

                    353

                    The Legal Frame Work Order, which amended a constitution of Pakistan unilaterally was issued by_______________?

                    Correct Answer: Yahya Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Legal Framework Order, 1970 (LFO) was a decree issued by Gen. Agha Muhammad Yahya Khan.

                    It laid down the political principles and laws governing the 1970 general election,

                    which was the first direct election in the history of Pakistan.

                    The LFO also dissolved the "One Unit" scheme of West Pakistan

                    re-establishing the four provinces of:

                    • Punjab,
                    • Sindh
                    • Balochistan 
                    • the Northwest Frontier Province


                    ***

                    The Legal Framework Order, 2002 was issued by Pakistani President Pervez Musharraf in August 2002.


                    NA21-8-2023

                    Correct Answer: Five years
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The chief Justice of Pakistan will be appointed with the aid of the President, and every of the other Judges shall be appointed by the President after session with the leader Justice.
                    • a person shall now not be appointed a decide of the ultimate court except he is a citizen of Pakistan and has for a period of, or for periods aggregating, now not less than 5 years been a decide of a Supreme Court of Pakistan (which include a excessive court docket which existed in Pakistan at any time earlier than the commencing day);

                    387

                    In which year Queen Elizabeth II visited Pakistan for the first time?

                    Correct Answer: 1961
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Know everything about Queen Elizabeth II
                    • Queen Elizabeth died on 8 September 2022 in Scotland.
                    • Queen Elizabeth II was born in Mayfair, London on 21 April 1926.
                    • Elizabeth became Queen on 6 February 1952 after the death of her father King George.
                    • Elizabeth became Queen at the age of 25.
                    • Elizabeth administrated oath to the 15th Prime minister of UK Liz Truss on 6 September 2022 (2 days before death).
                    • Elizabeth II is the first female monarch to have the longest reign in British history.
                    • Elizabeth reigned over 70 years and 214, longest of any British monarch.
                    • Elizabeth II was also the queen of 14 other commonwealth realms in addition to the UK.
                    • Elizabeth visited Pakistan in 1961 & 1997.
                    • Elizabeth II also remained monarch of Pakistan from 6 February 1952 to 23 March 1956.
                    • Her eldest son Charles, automatically became king of the United Kingdom and the head of state of 14 other realms including Australia, Canada and New Zealand.
                    388

                    When Balochistan state became a part of the West Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: 14 Oct 1955
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In 1955, Balochistan was merged into one unit of West Pakistan.

                    After the dissolution of one-Unit, Balochistan emerged as one of the four new provinces of Pakistan.

                    389

                    Which is the era of Alliance in Pakistan’s foreign policy______________?

                    Correct Answer: 1954-1962
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Period: 1954–1962
                    • Known as the main “era of alliances” in Pakistan’s foreign policy
                    • Pakistan built a formal security partnership with the United States during the Cold War
                    • Joined key defense blocs:
                      • SEATO (1954)
                      • CENTO (1955)
                    • Objective: Strengthen defense and counter communism

                    ****

                    Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

                    390

                    In which year Queen Elizabeth II made her last visit to Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: 1997
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Know everything about Queen Elizabeth II
                    • Queen Elizabeth died on 8 September 2022 in Scotland.
                    • Queen Elizabeth II was born in Mayfair, London on 21 April 1926.
                    • Elizabeth became Queen on 6 February 1952 after the death of her father King George.
                    • Elizabeth became Queen at the age of 25.
                    • Elizabeth administrated oath to the 15th Prime minister of UK Liz Truss on 6 September 2022 (2 days before death).
                    • Elizabeth II is the first female monarch to have the longest reign in British history.
                    • Elizabeth reigned over 70 years and 214, longest of any British monarch.
                    • Elizabeth II was also the queen of 14 other commonwealth realms in addition to the UK.
                    • Elizabeth visited Pakistan in 1961 & 1997.
                    • Elizabeth II also remained monarch of Pakistan from 6 February 1952 to 23 March 1956.
                    • Her eldest son Charles, automatically became king of the United Kingdom and the head of state of 14 other realms including Australia, Canada and New Zealand.

                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    • Pakistan came into being on 14 August 1947.
                    • Note: 20th century began on January 1, 1901 and ended on December 31, 2000.

                    The then US President Jimmy Carter administration cut off the military and economic aid to Pakistan in November 1977.

                    395

                    When Zakat system was introduced in Pakistan_____?

                    Correct Answer: 1980
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Zakat and Ushr began in 1980 with an ordinance decreed by General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq.

                    Zakat became compulsory during the 2nd hijrah but its complete system was imposed in the year of the 8th hijrah.

                    The word Zakat occurs in the Holy Quran 32 Times

                    The word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs 22 times

                    Parents, Husbands, wife and children are not entitled to get Zakat.

                    ND31-12-2022

                    397

                    Who was the founder of FIA (Federal Investigation Agency) Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was the founder of FIA (Federal Investigation Agency) Pakistan
                    • ****
                    • FIA of Pakistan was established on January 13, 1975
                    • ****
                    • It was established under FIA Act, 1974 (Act-VIII of 1975) promulgated on 13-01-1975.
                    • FIA stands for Federal Investigation Agency
                    • ****

                    • ****The operational structure of FIA****
                    • Headquarters----> Islamabad, Pakistan
                    • Agency executive-->Mohsin Hassan Butt[1], Director-General

                    398

                    Pervez Musharraf resigned as Army Chief in the year_?

                    Correct Answer: 2007
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Know everything about Musharraf

                    Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

                    Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

                    Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

                    Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

                    Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

                    Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

                    In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

                    In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

                    In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

                    Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

                    Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

                    Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

                    Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

                    In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

                    He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

                    On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

                    Died: 5 February 2023

                    Dying place: Dubai, UAE

                    Disease: amyloidosis

                    Aged: 79

                    Buried: Karachi

                    Correct Answer: Both

                    Explanation

                    It was passed on 4 may 1974

                    400

                    Who founded the Pakistan Times?

                    Correct Answer: Mian Iftikharuddin
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    It was owned and operated by Mian Iftikharuddin, a Punjabi politician formerly of the Indian National Congress but of All-India Muslim League after 1946.

                    The newspaper started publication on 4 February 1947. Its editor-in-chief in the 1940s was the communist poet Faiz Ahmed Faiz.

                    410

                    Which Association merged into Muslim League?

                    Correct Answer: Muhammadan Association
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    B

                    ANS:

                    Indian legal adviser and Shii pioneer. Taught at Hooghly College, Calcutta, and in London. Addressed in Islamic law at Calcutta College and was named to the Bengal Administrative Committee ( 1881 ) and the Bengal Tall Court ( 1890 ). Set up the Central National Muhammadan Affiliation ( 1877 ) and the All-India Muslim Association in London ( 1908 ). A productive creator who composed English-language books and articles on religion and history, contending Islam as the vehicle of levelheadedness and dynamism amid the age of European brutality and Muhammad as a courier of ethical humanism and advance. His most well-known book is Soul of Islam

                    • This set off a battle of succession amongst his four sons wherein his third son, Aurangzeb, emerged successful and usurped his father's throne.

                    Show explanation

                    **

                    NWFP was renamed "Khyber Pakhtunkhwa" on  19 April 2010 after 18th amendment of the constitution of Pakistan with 90 votes.

                    415

                    The “Tiger of Mysore” is called____________?

                    Correct Answer: Tipu Sultan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Tipu Sultan, known as the dreaded ‘Tiger of Mysore’, was a legend during his lifetime and is still regarded as an enlightened ruler in India.

                    During the late eighteenth century he bitterly and effectively opposed British rule in southern India, posing a grave threat to the East India Company.

                    Father of the nuclear bomb name A.Q khan

                    Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998 at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

                    5 Nuclear tests were conducted by Pakistan at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

                    The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District of Balochistan Province.

                    Chagai-I was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

                    while 

                    Chagai II

                    Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

                    These tests followed the Chagai-I series

                    The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

                    The number of tests is 1

                    Explanation

                    Qutab ud Din Aibak was the first ruler of the Muslim dynasty in Sub-Continent.

                    ......

                    Qutb al-Din Aibak was a general of Muhammad Ghori.

                    Qutab ud Din Aibak was in charge of the Ghurid territories in northern India, and after Muhammad Ghori's death.

                    He became the ruler of an independent kingdom that evolved into the Delhi Sultanate ruled by the Mamluk dynasty.

                    Qutab ud Din Aibak is buried in Lahore. 

                    ....

                    Al-khafif was the first ruler of soomra dynasty (1011-1026).

                    Mughal emperor Akbar the first Muslim ruler who tried to reconcile the differences between Muslims and Hindus.
