                    431

                    Acceptance of separate electorate which vindicated the________ became the basis of Muslim freedom struggle.

                    Correct Answer: Two Nation Theory
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The unification of bengal in 1911 brought back the haunting reality to Muslims that, while not a separate motherland frozen in two Nation Theory, they might be subject to the tyranny of the majority, in enlightenment thinker John Stuart Mill’s phrasing,72 as a result of the Muslims would be too spread and too few in variety across the landmass to gift their positions in an exceedingly megastate that might swallow them as minorities.
                    432

                    A London Muslim League delegation headed by Syed Ameer Ali met John Morley, the secretary of State for demands including:

                    Correct Answer: Separate electorate
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Separate electorate

                    The All-India Muslim League (advanced as the Muslim Alliance) was a political party built up in 1906 in British India.

                    Its solid promotion, from 1930 onwards, for the foundation of a isolated Muslim-majority nation-state, Pakistan, effectively driven to the segment of India in 1947 by the British Realm and include separate electorate.

                    433

                    Who was the Prime Minister of the Britain during Indo-Pak Partition?

                    Correct Answer: Clement Attlee
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The then British Prime Minister Attlee appointed Lord Louis Mountbatten as India's last viceroy, giving him the task to oversee British India's independence by June 1948, with the instruction to avoid partition and preserve a United India, but with adaptable authority to ensure a British withdrawal with minimal setbacks.


                    434

                    Simla Conference was held between all parties of the Subcontinent at Simla in:

                    Correct Answer: 1945
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Simla Conference of 1945 was a meeting between the Viceroy of India Lord Wavell and the major political leaders of British India at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla.

                    435

                    When Pakistan came into being how much assets were paid to Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: 20 Cror
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Indian government's cash balance at the time of the Partition were a little under Rs 400 crore and Pakistan's share was fixed at Rs 75 crore, which was inclusive of Rs 20 crore made available to Pakistan.

                    436

                    Pakistan came into being 27 Ramadan_________A.H?

                    Correct Answer: 1366 A.H
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    On 14 August 1947, Pakistan achieved independence one day prior to Indian independence.

                    India was partitioned, and an East and West Pakistan were created from Muslim majority areas.

                    • ****

                    441

                    The Aryans came from the ___________ in 1500 BC.

                    Correct Answer: Central Asia
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Aryans were a semi-nomadic pastoral people. By 1500 BCE the Aryans migrated into the Indian subcontinent. Coming from central Asia, this large group of nomadic cattle herders crossed the Hindu Kush Mountains and came in contact with the Indus Valley Civilization.

                    The poor farmers in Bengal took up indigo cultivation because:

                    Correct Answer: They were forced to do so

                    The East India Company was an English, and later British, joint-stock company.

                    It was established on December 31, 1600.

                    Ceased operations: June 1, 1874

                    Headquarters: London, United Kingdom

                    Founders: Thomas Smythe, Royal charter, John Watts

                    Defunct: 1 June 1874; 149 years ago

                    Note: The Delhi Proposals (1927) are often confused with the Nehru Report (1928).

                    449

                    Indian Civil Services was introduced during the rule of-----?

                    Correct Answer: Lard Cornwallis
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Lord Cornwallis, one of the Governor Generals of Bengal, introduced Civil Services to India.


                    The Indian Civil Service (ICS), officially known as the Imperial Civil Service

                    The Indian Civil Service (ICS) was the higher civil service of the British Empire in India during British rule in the period between 1858 and 1947.

                    Its members ruled over more than 300 million people

                    The Indian Civil Service (ICS) was appointed under Section XXXII(32) of the Government of India Act 1858

                    451

                    The English was permitted to trade in India (in Bengal):

                    Correct Answer: 1634
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The company, below such apparent patronage, quickly controlled to eclipse the Portuguese, who had installed their bases in Goa and Bombay (which became later ceded to England as a part of the dowry of Catherine de Braganza).
                    • It controlled to create strongholds in Surat (where a manufacturing unit become constructed in 1612), Madras (1639), Bombay (1668) and Calcutta (1690). by means of 1647, the organisation had 23 factories and ninety employees in India.
                    • The major factories have become the walled forts of fortress William in Bengal, fortress St George in Madras and the Bombay castle. In 1634, the Mughal emperor prolonged his hospitality to the English buyers to the place of Bengal (and in 1717 absolutely waived customs responsibilities for the alternate). The organisation's mainstay corporations were via now in cotton, silk, indigo, saltpeter and tea.

                    Sir Syed Ahmed Khan established a Translation Society (later, renamed as ‘Scientific society’) in 1864. In which town was it founded__________?

                    Ghazipur is a city in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India.

                    Ghazipur city is the administrative headquarters of the Ghazipur district,

                    one of the four districts that form the Varanasi division of Uttar Pradesh.

                    Sir Syed Ahmed Khan founded Scientific Society in 1864.

                    Sir Syed Ahmed Khan established a Translation Society (later, renamed as ‘Scientific society’).

                    In which town was it founded Ghazipur.

                    453

                    Congress agree separate electorate for Muslims in?

                    Correct Answer: 1919
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Congress agree separate electorate for Muslims in 1919.

                    In December 1916, AIML and Congress met in Lucknow.

                    It was due to untiring efforts of Jinnah that the Congress “agreed to separate electorate, for the first and the last time”.

                    5 To applaud these efforts of Jinnah, he was given the title of 'Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim unity' by Sarojni Naidu.

                    Correct Answer: Muhammad bin Qasim
                    Show explanation

                    The first permanent Muslim foothold in the subcontinent was achieved with Muhammad bin Qasim's conquest of Sindh in 711/712 A.D.

                    Who founded a college in Ajmer for children of princely rulers?

                    Correct Answer: Lord Mayo

                    457

                    According to Lucknow pact, the total members of counsels of large provinces would be:

                    Correct Answer: 125
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Lucknow Pact, agreement made by the Indian National Congress headed by leader Bal Gangadhar Tilak and the All-India Muslim League led by Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

                    it was adopted by the Congress at its Lucknow session on December 29 and by the league on Dec. 31, 1916.

                    458

                    Ameer Khasroo was the first poet of

                    Correct Answer: Urdu
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ameer Khusro is considered as the first poet of Pashto and Urdu

                    The Full name of Amīr Khusrau : Ab'ul Hasan Yamīn ud-Dīn K͟husrau

                    Khusrau is credited for the invention of the sitar.

                    Amīr Khusrau lived under the Delhi Sultanate. 

                    Amīr Khusrau  Born: 1253, Patiyali

                    Amīr Khusrau  Died: on October 1325, in Delhi, India

                    The Place of burial of Amīr Khusrau: Hazrat Nizamuddin Aulia Dargah, New Delhi, India

                    while

                    Baba Farid is the first Punjabi poet.

                    Allama Iqbal is the National poet of Pakistan

                    Hassan Bin Sabit was the first poet of Islam

                    ND21-2-2023



                    459

                    Which of the following movement started a protest against the partition of Bengal in 1905?

                    Correct Answer: Swadeshi movement
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    History:

                    The Swadeshi movement was launched to reverse the: Partition of Bengal

                    After the British Government's decision for the partition of Bengal in 1903, the Swadeshi movement was formally started in Calcutta in 1905

                    Bengal was reunited by Lord Hardinge in 1911, in response to the Swadeshi movement's riots in protest against the policy
                    **
                    partition of Bengal

                    Lord Curzon divided Bengal into two provinces on 16-10-1905

                    on October 16, 1905, by Lord Curzon, the British colonial administrator of India at the time

                    The partition of Bengal was the most important event during the rule of Lord Curzon

                    The province was divided into West Bengal, which had a majority of Hindus,

                    and East Bengal, which had a majority of Muslims

                    ****

                    nd9-2-2023



                    460

                    Delhi captured by British in how many days

                    Correct Answer: 3 days
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Battle of Delhi was fought on 11 Sep 1803.

                    The British troops were under the leadership of General Lak

                    And Marathas of Scindia's army was under General Louis Bourquin

                    The battle was fought at Patparganji across the Yamuna River.

                    Finally, the city of Delhi fell three days later.

                    The modern city (present Delhi) developed only after 1911.

                    The period from 1803 to 1857 is called the Delhi Renaissance.


                    461

                    Who was appointed the first principal of Darul ulum Deoband_______?

                    Correct Answer: Maulana Muhammad Qasim
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    About Deoband Madrasah


                    Founded: 31 May 1866

                    Founder: Muhammad Qasim Nanautavi

                    First Principal: Maulana Muhammad Qasim

                    Location: Saharanpur, Uttar Pardesh

                     

                    462

                    Darul Uloom Deoband was founded on ________?

                    Correct Answer: 31 May 1866
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Deoband Madrasah, founded by Muhammad Qasim Nanautavi on 31 May 1866.
                      • It is located in Saharanpur, Uttar Pradesh.
                      • Its first principal was Maulana Muhammad Qasim.

                      Who was the President of USA at the time of creation of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Harry Truman
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      464

                      By he early eighteenth century , the east India company had three main bases:

                      Correct Answer: Bombay,Calcutta and Madras
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The East India Company was an English, and later British, joint-stock company founded in 1600 and dissolved in 1874.

                      Founded: December 31, 1600

                      Founders: Elizabeth I of England, Thomas Smythe, John Watts, Royal charter

                      Headquarters: London, United Kingdom

                      Defunct: 1 June 1874; 148 years ago

                      *********


                      465

                      British Cabinet Mission visit India, Interim government formed at the Centre:

                      Correct Answer: 1946
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      A Cabinet Mission came to India in 1946 in order to discuss the transfer of power from the British government to the Indian political leadership, with the aim of preserving India's unity and granting its independence.

                      466

                      Bicameralism was introduced in India under:

                      Correct Answer: Government of India act 1919
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Bicameralism was introduced in India under Government of India act 1919.
                      • In 1918, Edwin Montagu, the Secretary of State, and Lord Chelmsford, the Viceroy, produced their scheme of constitutional reforms, known as the Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms, which led to the enactment of the Government of India Act of 1919.
                      467

                      Who was the Governor-General of India during the Sepoy Mutiny?

                      Correct Answer: Charles John Canning,Earl Canning,
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Indian Mutiny, also called Sepoy Mutiny or First War of Independence

                      Indian Mutiny was a widespread but unsuccessful rebellion against British rule in India in 1857–59.

                      Location: Agra Delhi India Kanpur Lucknow

                      Participants: Mughal dynasty United Kingdom

                      Charles John Canning, Earl Canning was the governor-general of India during the Indian Mutiny of 1857.

                      468

                      The earliest Surat factories were established by the?

                      Correct Answer: English
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The earliest factories in Surat were established by the English East India Company.

                      They were granted permission to set up a factory in Surat in 1612 by the Mughal Emperor Jahangir. 

                      Founded: 31 December 1600

                      Founders

                      • William Cavendish
                      • George Clifford
                      • John Harte
                      • James Lancaster
                      • John Spencer
                      • John Watts (et. al.)

                      Defunct: 1 June 1874

                      Headquarters: East India House, London, Great Britain


                      By 1623, the English East India Company had established factories at :

                      • Surat
                      • Broach
                      • Ahmedabad
                      • Agra
                      • Masulipatam
                      469

                      Who issued Comrade English newspaper from Calcutta?

                      Correct Answer: Mualana Muhammad Ali Johar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      the famous newspaper "Comrade and Hamdard" was issued Ch Rehmat Ali 


                      Mualana Muhammad Ali Johar issued Comrade English newspaper from Calcutta


                      Maulana launched his famous English weekly “Comrade” from Calcutta in 1911 and his Urdu weekly “Hamdard” from Dehli in 1913.

                       

                      470

                      Pakistan independence movement started in London by

                      Correct Answer: Chaudhary Rehmat ali
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Pakistan National Movement was founded in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali who is also credited with creating the name “Pakistan”, for a separate Muslim homeland in South Asia.

                      After publishing the declaration Now or Never, he felt the need of putting up a co-operative effort to publicize and promote his ideas. 

                      471

                      The British East Indian company rule came to end in the year-------------.

                      Correct Answer: 1858
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The British East Indian company rule came to end in the year  1858

                      **

                       the rule of the East India Company on the Indian subcontinent from 1773 to 1858.

                      For rule by the British Crown from 1858 to 1947, see British Raj.

                      • 1774–1785 (first) Warren Hastings

                      • 1857–1858 (last)Charles Canning

                      ****
                      British East India Company Founder John Watts

                      British East India Company was Founded on December 31, 1600

                      Headquarters--------->East India House, London, Great Britain

                      Ceased operations June 1, 1874

                      ***


                      472

                      Which Muslim leader had died at the time of second round table conference ?

                      Correct Answer: Muhammad Ali Johar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first Round Table Conference convened from 12 November 1930 to 19 January 1931

                      • ****
                      • The Second Round table conference was held from 7 September 1931 to 1 December 1931 in London. 
                      • ****
                      • The Third Round Table Conference was held in 1932 between November and December. 

                      • ****

                      • First round table conference: 1930

                        2nd: 1931

                        3rd:1932

                        All in London

                      • All three round table conferences were attended by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and Tej Bahadur Sapru.

                      Who negotiated with cabinet mission (1946) on behalf of All India National Congress________?

                      Correct Answer: A.K.Azad
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Formulated at the initiative of Clement Attlee the mission had Lord Pethick-Lawrence Sir Stafford Cripps and A.V. Alexander.

                      474

                      Wavell plan presented in which conference ?

                      Correct Answer: Simla conference
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Shimla Conference of 1945 was a meeting between the Viceroy of India Lord Wavell and the major political leaders of British India at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla.
                      475

                      ARY News is a Pakistani news channel launched on__________?

                      Correct Answer: 2004
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      ARY News is a Pakistani news channel launched on 26 September 2004 with the name "ARY One World" until 2009 when it was rebranded as "ARY News". 

                      476

                      A disease that spreads by contact:

                      Correct Answer: Contagious
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      A disease that spreads by contact: Contagious
                      Contagious diseases (such as the flu, colds, or strep throat) spread from person to person in several ways.
                      two reasons for spreading contagious disease:
                      One way is through direct physical contact
                      another way is infectious microbe travels through the air

                      478

                      The world longest glacial system outside the polar regions (Biafo Glacier) is located in _______ ?

                      Correct Answer: Pakistan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The world longest glacial system outside the polar regions (Biafo Glacier) is located in Pakistan.

                      Biafo Glacier length is 67 kilometres (42 mi).

                      Biafo Glacier Located in Karakoram, Hispar Valley, Pakistan

                      Rupal Glacier other name is Tashain Glacier.

                      Rupal Glacier or Tashain Glacier located in Pakistan.

                      The length of Baltur Glacier is ______ ?

                      The length of Baltur Glacier is 62KM (39 mi)

                      one of the longest glaciers outside the polar regions.

                      It is located in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of Pakistan.

                      It runs through part of the Karakoram mountain range.

                      485

                      Which Glacier is the 2nd Largest Glacier of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Batura
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The Baltoro Glacier is the second-longest glacier in Pakistan
                      • Length 64.5 kilometers long,
                      • Location: Gojal region of Gilgit-Baltistan in Pakistan
                      • Located in the Karakoram Mountains.

                      Additional information 

                      • 1st Largest: Siachen Glacier (approx. 75 km, contested with India).

                      Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

                      486

                      How much area of Mountain region is covered by glaciers in Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: 13%
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      13% Mountain region is covered by glaciers in Pakistan.

                      In Pakistan the total area covered by glaciers is 1369 sq.km

                      Physiographic distribution can be expressed in percentage of the total area as follows: 30 % Mountains 27.7% Plateaus 

                      58% area of Pakistan is covered by Mountains and plateaus 

                      42% by plains and detests

                      Almost 60% of the geographical area of Pakistan is rangeland.

                      489

                      Which of the following glaciers are present in Karakoram range?

                      Correct Answer: All of these
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Following glacier located in Karakoram range.

                      1.      Siachen Glacier, Pakistan

                      2.      Baltoro Glacier, Pakistan

                      3.      Hispar Glacier, Pakistan

                      4.      Batura Glacier, Pakistan

                      5.      Biafo Glacier, Pakistan

                      6.      Chogo Lungma Glacier, Pakistan

                      7.      Yinsugaiti Glacier, China

                      Following glacier located in Himalayas range.

                       the Gangotri and Yamunotri (Uttarakhand)

                      2.      Khumbu glaciers (Mount Everest region),

                      3.      Langtang glacier (Langtang region)

                      4.      Zemu (Sikkim).

                      Following glacier located in Karakoram range.

                      2.      Baltoro Glacier, Pakistan

                      3.      Hispar Glacier, Pakistan

                      4.      Batura Glacier, Pakistan

                      3.      Langtang glacier (Langtang region)

                      4.      Zemu (Sikkim).

                      491

                      The Government of India Act 1935 comprised __________ Sections and ___________ Schedules.

                      Correct Answer: 321, 10
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The Government of India Act, 1935 was an Act adapted from the Parliament of the United Kingdom.
                      • Act of 1935 consisted of 321 sections.
                      • Act of 1935 contained 10 schedules.

                      492

                      The most important feature of the Government of India Act of 1919 was the introduction of

                      Correct Answer: Dyarchy
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Government of India Act of 1919 was passed by the British Parliament to introduce diarchy into the state government.

                      Reason The Montagu-Chelmsford Reform Commission has recommended the introduction of diarchy into the state government.

                      Diarchy, also written diarchy, a dual government system introduced in the Indian state of England by the Government of India Act (1919).

                      Correct Answer: Karachi

                      Manora is also famous for Naval Base.

                      Manora is located near Karachi.

                      Which Congress leader thought after Lahore Resolution that partition was unavoidable:

                      Correct Answer: Raj Gopal Acharia
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Who wrote the first draft of Pakistan Resolution?

                      Correct Answer: Zafarullah Khan

                      Explanation

                      500

                      The Lahore Resolution was amended and Pakistan was made an integral part of League’s objective. The amendment was made in the Annual session (1941) of All India Muslim League, which was held at__________?

                      Correct Answer: Madras
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      On 15 April 1941, the Lahore Resolution was incorporated as a creed in the constitution of the All-India Muslim League in its Madras session

                      while

                      On March 23, A.K. Fazul Haq, the Chief Minister of Bengal, moved the historical Lahore Resolution

                      The resolution was presented at Minto Park, in Lahore, by Maulvi A.K. Fazlul Huq

                      Lahore Resolution Translation English to Urdu: Maulana Zafar Ali Khan

                      Lahore Resolution Presented by A.K Fazul Haq        

                      Participate From Punjab (Leader): Zafar Ali Khan

                      Participate From Sindh (Leader): Sir Abdullah Haroon

                      Participate From Balochistan (Leader): Qazi Faiz Isa

                      Participate From KPK (Leader): Sardar Aurangzaib Khan

                      CM Banglad : A.K Fazul Haq 

                      United Province: Khalid Zaman
